X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

Aruba802dot11kBcnRptReq_BcnRptReqProfile.description:
The Beacon Report Requests are sent only to 802.11k-compliant clients that advertise Beacon Report Capability in their
Radio Measurement (RM) Enabled Capabilities Information Element (IE).
This includes configuring BSSID, Channel, Measurement Mode, Regulatory Class, etc.

profile.description:
Configures list of Beacon Report Request Profile to provide the parameters for the Beacon Report Request frames.
The Beacon Report Request profile is attached to 802.11K profile.

bssid.description: BSSID included in the beacon request. Default: wildcard

channel.description:
This option is used to set the Channel field in the Beacon Report Request frame. The Channel value can be set to one of the following:
The channel of the AP (when Measurement Mode is set to either 'Passive' or 'Active-All channels') 0 (when Measurement Mode is set to 'Beacon Table')
255 (when Measurement Mode is set to 'Active-Channel Report')

oneOf.x-typeDescription: 2.4GHz radio channel list
5GHz radio channel list
6GHz radio channel list

x-enumDescriptions.ZERO_CHAN_FOR_ZERO: some times 0 is also an valid input

oneOf.x-typeDescription: Channel 0/255 for all channels

include-ssid.description: Enable including SSID IE in Beacon Report Request

last-beacon-rpt-indication.description: Enable Last Beacon Request Indication sub-element in Beacon Report Request.

measure-duration.description:
Measurement Duration field in the Beacon Report Request frame.
The Measurement Duration is set to the duration of the requested measurement. It is expressed in units of TUs.

measure-mode.description: Measurement Mode for Beacon Reports. The mode should be one of active-all-ch|active-ch-rpt|beacon-table|passive. Default: beacon-table
measure-mode.x-typeDescription: Indicates the mode used for the measurement.

random-interval.description:
Randomization Interval field in the Beacon Report Request frame.
The Randomization Interval is used to specify the desired maximum random delay in the measurement start time.
It is expressed in units of TUs (Time Units). A Randomization Interval of 0 in a measurement request indicates that
no random delay is to be used.

reg-class.description: Regulatory Class for Beacon Report Request. Value: auto, 1, 12, 81, 115, or 131. Default auto to use value based on country code

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: Auto uses value based on country code.

reg-class.x-typeDescription: to specify the Regulatory Class field in the Beacon Report Request frame.

report-detail.description: Value for the Detail field in the Reporting Detail sub-element present in the Beacon Report Request frame.

request-info.description:
Contents of the Request Information IE that could be present in the Beacon Report Request frame.
The Request Information IE is present for all Measurement Modes except the Beacon Table mode. It consists of a list of Element IDs that
should be included by the client in the response frame.
Any valid element ID in the x/y/z format. For example, 0/21/22.

bssid.description: BSSID included in the beacon request. Default: wildcard

Aruba802dot11kRrmIe_RrmIeProfilesSchemaG.description:
The Radio Resource Management (RRM) Information Element (IE) is essential for optimizing radio resource allocation in wireless networks.
It enhances network performance by managing resources efficiently and ensuring quality of service.

profile.description: Configures list of Radio Resource Management (RRM) IE profile to define the information elements advertised by an AP with 802.11k support enabled.

country-ie.description: Enable advertising in beacon and probe responses the device's regulatory domain

enable-capabilities-ie.description: Enable advertising in beacon and probe responses support for radio measurements in a device.

enable-capabilities-ie-civic.description: In beacons and probe responses, enables the bit of civic measurement support in the RM Enabled Capabilities IE.

enable-capabilities-ie-lci.description: In beacons and probe responses, enables the bit of LCI measurement support in the RM Enabled Capabilities IE.

country-ie.description: Enable advertising in beacon and probe responses the device's regulatory domain

enable-capabilities-ie.description: Enable advertising in beacon and probe responses support for radio measurements in a device.

enable-capabilities-ie-civic.description: In beacons and probe responses, enables the bit of civic measurement support in the RM Enabled Capabilities IE.

enable-capabilities-ie-lci.description: In beacons and probe responses, enables the bit of LCI measurement support in the RM Enabled Capabilities IE.

country-ie.description: Enable advertising in beacon and probe responses the device's regulatory domain

enable-capabilities-ie.description: Enable advertising in beacon and probe responses support for radio measurements in a device.

enable-capabilities-ie-civic.description: In beacons and probe responses, enables the bit of civic measurement support in the RM Enabled Capabilities IE.

enable-capabilities-ie-lci.description: In beacons and probe responses, enables the bit of LCI measurement support in the RM Enabled Capabilities IE.

Aruba802dot11k_Dot11kProfilesSchemaGET.description:
The 802.11k protocol provides mechanisms for APs and clients to dynamically measure the available radio resources.
In an 802.11k enabled network, APs and clients can send neighbor reports, beacon reports, and link measurement reports to each other.
This allows the APs and clients to take appropriate connection actions.
This includes configuring channel-report, reference of Beacon Report Request profile and RRM IE Profile, etc.

profile.description: Configures list of 802.11k radio profile. The 802.11k profile must be attached to the WLAN SSID.

bcn-rpt-req-profile-name.description: Beacon Report Request Settings for the selected profile.

beacon-req-time.description: Set time duration between consecutive Beacon Requests. Range: 10-200 sec, Default: 60 sec, Turn-Off: 0

channel-report-11a.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 5GHz band. Range: [34-173], Default: 36

channel-report-11bg.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 2.4GHz band. Range: [1-14], Default: 1

channel-report-6ghz.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 6GHz band. Range: [1-233], Default: 1

enable.description: Enables the 802.11K feature. This feature is disabled by default.

nb-resp-wide-band-ie.description: Include the wide channel bandwidth information element in the neighbor report responses, when the dot11k setting is enabled on the managed device.

bcn-rpt-req-profile-name.description: Beacon Report Request Settings for the selected profile.

beacon-req-time.description: Set time duration between consecutive Beacon Requests. Range: 10-200 sec, Default: 60 sec, Turn-Off: 0

channel-report-11a.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 5GHz band. Range: [34-173], Default: 36

channel-report-11bg.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 2.4GHz band. Range: [1-14], Default: 1

channel-report-6ghz.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 6GHz band. Range: [1-233], Default: 1

enable.description: Enables the 802.11K feature. This feature is disabled by default.

nb-resp-wide-band-ie.description: Include the wide channel bandwidth information element in the neighbor report responses, when the dot11k setting is enabled on the managed device.

bcn-rpt-req-profile-name.description: Beacon Report Request Settings for the selected profile.

channel-report-11bg.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 2.4GHz band. Range: [1-14], Default: 1

channel-report-6ghz.description: Set channel for AP Channel Reports in 6GHz band. Range: [1-233], Default: 1

enable.description: Enables the 802.11K feature. This feature is disabled by default.

nb-resp-wide-band-ie.description: Include the wide channel bandwidth information element in the neighbor report responses, when the dot11k setting is enabled on the managed device.

ArubaAaaCaptivePortal_CaptivePortalSchem.description:
Use this API to configure external captive portal profiles for guest users. For AP and GW, When the captive portal profile is applied to an SSID or a wired profile, the users connecting
to the SSID or wired network are assigned a role with the captive portal rule. For CX, when the client is 802.1x or MAC authenticated through RADIUS-server, the redirect parameters in this
profile can be applied through user-role, Downloadable User Role and RADIUS Vendor Specific Attribute. This feature is applicable for AP, CX and GW.

items.description: Allow List of Netdestinations.

ap-mac-in-redirection-url.description: Add AP's MAC address in redirection URL.

apple-cna-bypass.description: To bypass Apple CNA on iOS devices like iPad, iPhone, iPod.

auth-text.description:
Configures the authentication text to be returned by the external server. The authentication text command configuration is
required only for the External Authentication Text splash mode.

auto-allowlist.description: Disables automatic whitelisting / allowlisting of URLs.

items.description: Deny List of Netdestinations.

description.description: Description, or comment, for the Captive Portal profile.

enable-welcome-page.description: Enable the welcome page before redirection to original destination.

external-cp-server-url.description: The URL for the external captive portal server, URL format:http(s)://<server>:<port>/path.

ip-addr-in-redirection-url.description: Add one of the controller interfaces in the redirection URL.

logon-wait-cpu-threshold.description: Logon wait when CPU is overloaded, CPU utilization threshold (%). Range: 1-100. Default: 60.

logon-wait-max-delay.description: Logon wait when CPU is overloaded,Maximum wait (secs).

logon-wait-min-delay.description: Logon wait when CPU is overloaded, minimum wait (secs).

logout-popup-window.description: Open popup window for logout after authentication.

max-authentication-failures.description: Maximum auth failures before user is added to denylist.Range: 0-10. Default: 0.

name.description: The name of the captive portal profile.

out-of-service-page.description: Configures a URL to redirect the users when the internet uplink is down.

prevent-frame-overlay.description: When the Prevent frame overlay option is enabled, a frame can display a page only if it is in the same domain as the main page.

redirect-pause.description: Pause time for automatic redirect.Range: 1-60, 0 to disable. Default: 10.

redirect-url.description: Specify a redirect URL to redirect users to another URL.
redirect-url.x-typeDescription:
An ASCII-encoded Uniform Resource Locator (URL) as defined
in RFC 3986, section 1.1.3

server-failthrough.description: Allow the guest clients to access Internet when the external captive portal server is not available.

server-offload.description: Enables the server-offload feature to reduce the load on the external captive portal server by allowing the Instant AP to use a Meta tag to redirect HTTP and HTTPS requests from the client.

show-acceptable-use-policy.description: Show the acceptable use policy page before the logon page.

show-fqdn.description: Allow user to see and select FQDN.

single-session.description: Allow only one active user session at a time.

switch-ip.description: Sends the IP address of the virtual controller in the redirection URL when external captive portal servers are used.

url-hash-key-ciphertext-value.description: Hash the redirection URL using this key in ciphertext format.

url-hash-key-value.description: Hash the redirection URL using this key.
url-hash-key-value.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

use-https.description: Enforce clients to use HTTPS to communicate with the authentication server

user-idle-timeout.description: User idle timeout value. Valid range is 30-1296000 seconds in multiples of 30 seconds
user-idle-timeout.x-typeDescription: User idle timeout for Captive Portal in seconds. Valid range is 30-1296000 seconds in multiples of 30 seconds.

user-vlan-in-redirection-url.description: Add user VLAN in redirection URL.

welcome-page.description: Alternate welcome page to show after authentication.

user-idle-timeout.description: User idle timeout value. Valid range is 30-1296000 seconds in multiples of 30 seconds
user-idle-timeout.x-typeDescription: User idle timeout for Captive Portal in seconds. Valid range is 30-1296000 seconds in multiples of 30 seconds.

user-vlan-in-redirection-url.description: Add user VLAN in redirection URL.

welcome-page.description: Alternate welcome page to show after authentication.

eap-type.description: EAP method, default method is EAP-PEAP.

enable.description: Enable 802.1X authentication to be terminated on the controller. This option is disabled by default

x-enumDescriptions.EAP_GTC: EAP-GenericTokenCard as the inner authentication protocol.
x-enumDescriptions.EAP_MSCHAPV2: EAP-MSCHAPV2 as the inner authentication protocol.

eap-type.description: EAP method, default method is EAP-PEAP.

enable.description: Enable 802.1X authentication to be terminated on the controller. This option is disabled by default

x-enumDescriptions.EAP_GTC: EAP-GenericTokenCard as the inner authentication protocol.
x-enumDescriptions.EAP_MSCHAPV2: EAP-MSCHAPV2 as the inner authentication protocol.

auth-vlan-id.description:
Configures the VLAN where the port is moved after a successful
authentication.
auth-vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ca-cert.description: CA Certificate Name for the Client Certificate Verification.

cached-reauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) during which cached re-authentication
is allowed. This is applicable only when cached-reauth is enabled.

canned-eap-success-enable.description:
Allow the device to send a canned EAP success packet to the supplicant
to indicate a successful authentication when:
1. The authentication fails due to a RADIUS server reject, but
the interface on which the client on-boarded has a reject-role
configured.
2. The authentication fails due to a RADIUS server timeout, but
the interface on which the client on-boarded has a critical-role
configured.

cert-cn-lookup.description: Check certificate common name against AAA server.

client-limit.description: Maximum number of clients that can be authenticated on the interface.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTHORIZED:
Gives access to a device connected to the port even without a valid
802.1X crendential.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTO:
The device connected to the port must support 802.1X authentication
and provide valid credentials to get network access.
x-enumDescriptions.UNAUTHORIZED:
Deny access to a device connected to the port even with a valid
802.1X crendential.

delete-keycache.description: Delete key cache entry when user entry is deleted.

discovery-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait before an EAPoL request
identity frame re-transmission on an 802.1X enabled interface
with no authenticated client.

eapol-max-requests.description:
The number of EAPOL requests to send to a supplicant
that must time out before authentication fails and
the authentication session ends.

eapol-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a response from a client before
retransmitting an EAPoL PDU.

enable.description: Enable 802.1X authentication on a port.

enforce-suite-b-128.description: Enable Suite-B 128 bit or more security level Authentication enforcement.

enforce-suite-b-192.description: Enable Suite-B 192 bit or more security level Authentication enforcement.

heldstate-bypass.description: Maximum number of times station can send bad credentials

initial-auth-response-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for the first EAPoL frame from a
client before deeming the client to be incapable of 802.1X and
start authentication using the next configured authentication
method, if any.
If not set, the next configured authentication method is attempted only
after the authentication cycle of the current method is complete.

keycache-timeout.description: Per BSSID PMKSA (Pairwise Master Key Security Association) cache interval in hours.

logoff-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) after which a client will be considered
removed from the port for a lack of activity.

macsec.description:
Enables the switch to provision a MACsec channel
dynamically when the 802.1X client is authenticated using
an EAP method that supports mutual authentication.

enable.description: Enable EAP based MACsec.

mka-cak-length.description: Length of CAK to generate for MKA.

max-reauth.description: Max number of reauthentication attempts before giving up.

max-retries.description:
Number of authentication attempts before authentication fails.
GW supported max range is 5

multicast-key-rotation-period.description: Set the period between multicast key rotation.

multicast-keyrotation.description: Enable or Disable Multicast Key Rotation.

opp-key-caching.description:
Enable Opportunistic Key Caching (WPA2/WPA3 Only).By default, the 802.1X authentication profile enables a
cached pairwise master key (PMK) derived via a client and
an associated AP and used when the client roams to a new AP.
This allows clients faster roaming without a full 802.1x authentication.

quiet-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait before processing an authentication
request from a client that failed authentication.

reauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to enforce periodic re-authentication of
clients. This is applicable only when reauth is enabled

reauth-server-termination-action.description: Use the termination-action attribute from the Server.

server-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a server response to an
authentication request. If there is no response within the configured
time frame, the device assumes that the authentication attempt has
timed out.

tls-guest-access.description: Enable guest access for users with valid certificate.

tls-guest-role.description: The leaf will be deprecated. Please use role-tls-guest for assigning TLS Guest role.

unauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait before moving the interface to the
VLAN for unauthenticated clients.

unauth-vlan-id.description:

client.

unicast-key-rotation-period.description: Set the period between unicast key rotation.

unicast-keyrotation.description: Enable or Disable Unicast Key Rotation.

use-session-key.description: Use RADIUS Session Key as the unicast WEP key. This option is disabled by default.

use-static-key.description: Use Static Key as the Unicast/Multicast WEP key.This option is disabled by default.

validate-pmkid.description:
PMK (Pairwise Master Key) identifier (WPA2/WPA3 Only).
This parameter instructs the controller to check the pairwise master key (PMK) ID
sent by the client. When this option is enabled,
the client must send a PMKID in the associate or reassociate frame to indicate that it supports OKC or PMK caching;
otherwise, full 802.1x authentication takes place.

wep-key-retries.description: Set the Number of times Unicast/Multicast EAPOL Key Messages are sent to the station.

wep-key-size.description: Set the Dynamic WEP Key Size.

wpa-groupkey-delay.description: Delay between WPA unicast and multicast key exchange.

wpa-key-period.description: Set the time period between each WPA/WPA2/WPA3 key exchange.

wpa-key-retries.description: Set the Number of times WPA/WPA2/WPA3 Key Messages are retried.

wpa2-key-delay.description: Delay between EAP-Success and unicast key exchange.

auth-vlan-id.description:
Configures the VLAN where the port is moved after a successful
authentication.
auth-vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ca-cert.description: CA Certificate Name for the Client Certificate Verification.

cached-reauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) during which cached re-authentication

canned-eap-success-enable.description:
Allow the device to send a canned EAP success packet to the supplicant
to indicate a successful authentication when:
1. The authentication fails due to a RADIUS server reject, but
the interface on which the client on-boarded has a reject-role
configured.
2. The authentication fails due to a RADIUS server timeout, but
the interface on which the client on-boarded has a critical-role
configured.

cert-cn-lookup.description: Check certificate common name against AAA server.

ArubaAaaDot1xauth_Dot1xauthSchemaGET.description:
IEEE 802.1X is an IEEE Standard for port-based Network Access Control (PNAC).
This standard provides an authentication mechanism to devices wishing to attach
to a LAN or WLAN.
IEEE 802.1X defines the encapsulation of the Extensible Authentication Protocol
(EAP) over IEEE 802, which is known as EAP over LAN (EAPOL).

802.1X authentication involves three entities, a supplicant, an authenticator,
and an authentication server.

*Supplicant*: An entity that wants to get authenticated.
*Authenticator*: A network device, such as an Ethernet switch that authenticates
the Supplicant.
*Authentication Server*: Typically a host running software supporting the RADIUS
and EAP protocols that provides an authentication service to an authenticator.

Until the Supplicant is authenticated, 802.1X Authenticator allows only EAPOL
traffic through the port to which the Supplicant is connected.
Only after the authentication is successful, the authenticator allows normal
traffic from the Supplicant.

denylist-on-failure.description: Add the station to denylist if machine authentication fails.

enable.description: Enable enforcement of Machine Authentication

machine-default-role.description: Default role assigned on only completing Machine Authentication.

user-default-role.description: Configure the Role assigned on only completing User Authentication.

denylist-on-failure.description: Add the station to denylist if machine authentication fails.

enable.description: Enable enforcement of Machine Authentication

machine-default-role.description: Default role assigned on only completing Machine Authentication.

user-default-role.description: Configure the Role assigned on only completing User Authentication.

canned-eap-success.description:
Configure the device to accept an EAP success without going through
the complete authentication cycle.

discovery-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator
on the other end before considering the link to be non 802.1X capable
and opening the interface on the data-plane.
On a timeout, the device will not use the authentication result to
determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap.
If not configured, the device will wait for the 802.1X authentication
cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the
interface.

eapol-force-multicast.description:
Force the device to send only multicast EAPoL packets irrespective of
receiving unicast EAPoL packets from the authenticator.

eapol-method.description: EAP method to use for authentication.

x-enumDescriptions.EAP_MD5: MD5 hash function to generate hashed password

eapol-protocol-version.description:
EAPoL protocol version to use in EAPoL frames transmitted by the
device.

eapol-source-mac.description: The MAC address to use as source MAC in EAPoL frames.

Use the MAC address of the interface on which 802.1X is running as
the source MAC in EAPoL frames.
x-enumDescriptions.SYSTEM_MAC:
Use the MAC address of the device on which 802.1X is running as
the source MAC in EAPoL frames.

ArubaAaaMacauth_MacauthSchemaGET.description:
The MAC Authentication method grants access to a secure network by
authenticating devices for access to the network.

The switch uses the MAC address of the client in the configured
format as the identity to authenticate the client against a
RADIUS server.

name.description: Name of the MAC Auth profile.
Name of the MAC Auth profile.
Name of the MAC Auth profile.

ArubaAaaProfile_AllowFloodTrafficConfigG.description: Group for allow-flood-traffic configuration shared by aaa-profile and port-security.

allow-flood-traffic.description:
Enable transmission of flood traffic (broadcast, multicast and
unknown unicast) out authentication enabled interfaces with no
authenticated client.

allow-flood-traffic.description:

unknown unicast) out authentication enabled interfaces with no

auth-precedence.description:
Precedence order for port-access authentication. Auth-precedence must
contain either 0 or 2 elements. If empty, the default precedence
order of dot1x followed by mac-auth will be used.

items.description:
Precedence order for port-access authentication. Auth-precedence must
contain either 0 or 2 elements. If empty, the default precedence
order of dot1x followed by mac-auth will be used.

auth-priority.description:
Priority order for port-access authentication. Auth-priority must
contain either 0 or 2 elements. If not set, priority will follow
the 'auth-precedence' order.

items.description:
Priority order for port-access authentication. Auth-priority must
contain either 0 or 2 elements. If not set, priority will follow
the 'auth-precedence' order.

the device.

items.description:
The list of reasons when a client can be automatically logged off by
the device.

x-enumDescriptions.FINAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILURE:
The device automatically logs-off a client if it fails
authentication with all the enabled authentication methods.
The authentication failure reason should not be one of server-reject
or server-timeout server-reject or server-timeout.

x-enumDescriptions.ENABLED:
Client can move between interfaces without authentication itself
on the new interface.
x-enumDescriptions.SECURE_ENABLED:
Client can move between interfaces but only after authenticating
itself on the new interface.

concurrent-onboarding.description:
Enable authentication methods to start concurrently for faster
onboarding. If not set, clients are onboarded based on the order
specified by 'onboarding-precedence'.

controlled-direction.description:
Configure how traffic is controlled on non-authenticated ports: in both directions (ingress and egress) or ingress only.

auth-mode.description:
The mode determining how each client on the interface can gain access
to the network.

x-enumDescriptions.CLIENT_MODE:
Each client must be authenticated separately to be granted network
access.
x-enumDescriptions.DEVICE_MODE:
The first authenticated client grants network access to all the other
clients.
x-enumDescriptions.MULTI_DOMAIN:
Limit the number of authenticated clients to one 'voice' client and a
configured number of 'data' clients.

cdp.description: Enable CDP packets to trigger authentication.

lldp.description: Enable LLDP packets to trigger authentication.

LLDP frame. This is applicable only when LLDP packets are
allowed to trigger authentication.

authentication is successful on the interface.

bpdu-proxy-logoff.description:
Proxy logoff the client based on the interface status in BPDU packets.
Device will clear authentication session of the client if it receives
an interface down status in the BPDU packet.
When not configured, no action is taken.

client-limit.description: Maximum number of clients that can be authenticated on the interface.

ArubaAaaProfile_AuthenticationLagAttribu.description: Group for authentication attributes which are supported on lag interface.

auth-mode.description:
The mode determining how each client on the interface can gain access
to the network.

x-enumDescriptions.CLIENT_MODE:
Each client must be authenticated separately to be granted network
access.
x-enumDescriptions.DEVICE_MODE:
The first authenticated client grants network access to all the other
clients.
x-enumDescriptions.MULTI_DOMAIN:
Limit the number of authenticated clients to one 'voice' client and a
configured number of 'data' clients.

cdp.description: Enable CDP packets to trigger authentication.

lldp.description: Enable LLDP packets to trigger authentication.

mac-type.description:
MAC address to use when authentication is triggered by an
LLDP frame. This is applicable only when LLDP packets are
allowed to trigger authentication.

x-enumDescriptions.CHASSIS_MAC: Use the chassis MAC in LLDP TLV
x-enumDescriptions.SOURCE_MAC: Use the source MAC in LLDP frame

bpdu-bypass.description:
BPDUs to be serviced by the device before authentication is
successful on the interface.

cdp.description:
Enable CDP packets to be serviced by the device before
authentication is successful on the interface.

lldp.description:
Enable LLDP packets to be serviced by the device before
authentication is successful on the interface.

bpdu-proxy-logoff.description:
Proxy logoff the client based on the interface status in BPDU packets.
Device will clear authentication session of the client if it receives
an interface down status in the BPDU packet.
When not configured, no action is taken.

client-limit.description: Maximum number of clients that can be authenticated on the interface.

auth-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients that have been authenticated
but not received any specific role or attribute from the authentication
server.
When not set, no role is applied for clients that successfully
authenticated without a role assigned from the RADIUS server.

auto-vlan-enable.description:
Enable automatic creation of a VLAN for authenticated clients if the
VLAN is not already available on the device.

cached-critical-role-enable.description:
Enables caching authenticated clients and their associated
role information on the interface. Clients are assigned to their
previously authenticated role.

critical-auth-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients, that failed authentication
due to unreachable authentication servers.
When not set, no role is applied for clients that could not
complete authentication due to RADIUS server being unreachable.

critical-data-vlan.description: Configure critical-auth VLAN as data VLAN.
critical-data-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

critical-voice-role.description:
The role to be applied to 'voice' client that have failed
re-authentication due to servers being unreachable.
When not set, no role will be applied for voice clients when they
fail re-authentication due to server being unreachable.

critical-voice-vlan.description: Configure critical-auth VLAN as voice VLAN.
critical-voice-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

x-enumDescriptions.NAMED_VLAN: Configure the VLAN(s) using a named-VLAN
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN_RANGES: Configure the VLAN(s) as a list of VLAN ranges

fallback-role.description:
The role that will be applied to clients that have not been
assigned a role via any of the onboarding methods.
When not set, no role is applied for the client.

open-data-vlan.description: Configure open-auth VLAN as data VLAN.
open-data-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

open-voice-vlan.description: Configure open-auth VLAN as voice VLAN.
open-voice-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

pre-auth-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients that are yet to be authenticated.
When not set, no role is applied for clients while the
authentication is in progress for the client.

radius-override-enable.description:
Enable RADIUS override support. When enabled, RADIUS attributes
received from the server will override corresponding attributes
configured in the role assigned to the client.

reject-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients that failed authentication.
When not set, for AOS-CX devices, no role is applied for clients that fail authentication.
A default denyall role is applied on AOS-S devices.

ubt-fallback-role.description:
For any client,for which the corresponding UBT zone reachability
is failed, ubt-fallback role is applied. The client on ubt-fallback
role is automatically reverted to the previous role whenever UBT zone
status is back to reachable state.

ArubaAaaProfile_SecurityViolationConfigG.description: Group for security violation configurations shared by aaa-profile and port-security.

action.description:
Action to take on a security violation (notify or shutdown).
This is only supported on physical ports.

x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Prevent an SNMP trap from being sent.
x-enumDescriptions.NOTIFY: Send an SNMP trap and log an event on a violation.
x-enumDescriptions.SHUTDOWN:
Send an SNMP trap, log an event and shut the interface down on a
violation.

recovery-timer.description:
Time in seconds after which the interface is re-enabled if it was
shutdown in response to a violation event.
This is only applicable if shutdown recovery is enabled.

shutdown-recovery-enable.description:
Enable auto-recovery of the interface. This is only applicable when
the violation action is set to shutdown.
The interface is re-enabled after the recovery timer has expired.

enable.description: Enable Stateful Dot1x Profile. Default is disabled.

timeout.description: Timeout period for request. Range: 1-20. Default: 10.

enable.description: Enable Stateful Dot1x Profile. Default is disabled.

timeout.description: Timeout period for request. Range: 1-20. Default: 10.

ArubaAaaStatefulDot1x_StatefulDot1xProfi.description:
Condigure a Stateful 802.1X profile.
This feature when enabled allows the controller to learn the identity and role of a user connected to a third-party AP,
and is useful for authenticating users to networks with APs from multiple vendors. This has been widely used in
SDWAN branch gateways to learn the roles of clients connected to IAP. When any 801.1X authenticator sends RADIUS request to server,
gateway inspects this request and its response from server to learn about the authentication state of the user. Gateway then applies an
identity-based user role to the device

cert-usage-assignment.description:
Configure the parameters of certificate assignments. A specific type of certificate can be assigned to multiple applications; however,
an application can only have one certificate of each type. Certificate types include CA certificate, server certificate, client certificate,
and public certificate.

app-type.description: Specify the application for which you want to configure a certificate.

x-enumDescriptions.AP1X: AP does 802.1x authentication as supplicant, assign certificate to AP1X
x-enumDescriptions.CLEARPASS: Assign certificate to ClearPass Policy Manager.
x-enumDescriptions.RADIUS: Assign certificate to Remote Authentication Dial In User Service(RADIUS).
x-enumDescriptions.WEBCC: Assign certificate to Web Content Control.

cert-name.description: Specify the name of the certificate. This name will be used to assign the certificate to an application.

sequence-id.description: The sequence id determines the cert assignment order.

authentication-type.description: Specifies authentication protocol which is used for communication with Auth servers. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS or TACACS.

deadtime.description: Dead time for unavailable authentication server.

dns-query-interval.description:
Configure the query interval in seconds.
This field can be configured only when the dns-query-mode is set to FIXED.

dns-query-mode.description:
Configure the query mode to resolve a FQDN address for a RADIUS server.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS

x-enumDescriptions.BACKOFF: Use exponential backoff wait timer for DNS queries.
x-enumDescriptions.FIXED: Use a fixed wait timer for DNS queries.

dynamic-authorization-enable.description: Dynamic Authorization Enable.This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

dynamic-authorization-port.description: UDP port number used by the dynamic authorization server. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS
dynamic-authorization-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

enable.description: Dynamic Authorization Enable.This field can be configured only when type is RFC3576.

enable-radsec.description: Enables RADIUS over TLS. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

infinite-deadtime.description: Infinite deadtime for the Auth server.

ordering-sequence.description: Enables reordering upon deleting of tacacs server.

retries.description: The number of retry attempts to use for reaching out to RADIUS servers. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

service-type-in-access-request.description: Service Type attribute in access request. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

shared-secret-config.description:
This passkey is used as shared-secret for encrypting the communication between all Auth servers and the switch.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS or TACACS

single-connection-mode.description: Single connection mode for TACACS servers. This field can be configured only when type is TACACS.

status-server-interval.description: Time interval to send the status server message. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

timeout.description:
The timeout value specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the Auth server before moving to next server.
For CX, range is 1-60. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS or TACACS.

tls-initial-connection-timeout.description: TLS Initial connection timeout (timeout for TLS handshake). This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

ArubaAuthServerGlobal_SharedSecretInfo.description: Group for global auth-server shared key.

ciphertext-value.description:
The global encrypted shared key used between the authentication
server and the device. This field can be configured only when secret-type is CIPHER_TEXT.

plaintext-value.description:
The global unencrypted shared key used between the authentication
server and the device. This field can be configured only when secret-type is PLAIN_TEXT.
plaintext-value.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

secret-type.description: Specify the secret key type: CIPHER_TEXT or PLAIN_TEXT.

server and the device. This field can be configured only when secret-type is CIPHER_TEXT.

The global unencrypted shared key used between the authentication
server and the device. This field can be configured only when secret-type is PLAIN_TEXT.
plaintext-value.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

secret-type.description: Specify the secret key type: CIPHER_TEXT or PLAIN_TEXT.

ArubaAuthServerGlobal_TrackingPasswordIn.description: Group for password of the user sent in the tracking request message.

ciphertext-value.description: Ciphertext for tracking password. This field can be configured only when password-type is CIPHER_TEXT.

password-type.description: Specify the tracking password type: CIPHER_TEXT or PLAIN_TEXT.

plaintext-value.description: Tracking plaintext password. This field can be configured only when password-type is PLAIN_TEXT.
plaintext-value.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

ArubaAuthServerGlobal_TrackingPasswordIn.description: Group for password of the user sent in the tracking request message.

ciphertext-value.description: Ciphertext for tracking password. This field can be configured only when password-type is CIPHER_TEXT.

password-type.description: Specify the tracking password type: CIPHER_TEXT or PLAIN_TEXT.

plaintext-value.description: Tracking plaintext password. This field can be configured only when password-type is PLAIN_TEXT.

A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

ArubaAuthServerGroup_ServerGroupsSchemaG.description:
Allow you to manage a configured authentication server to an ordered list in a server group, for AP and GW you can configure server rules to derive a user role,
VLAN ID or VLAN name from attributes returned by the server during authentication.
For CX, A maximum of 32 AAA server-groups are supported. Out of these four are default server-groups (local, none, radius, tacacs) and the rest 28 can be user configured.
For AP, A maximum of 16 AAA server-groups are supported for per device. And all server-groups can be user configured.
This feature is applicable for all devices.

fail-through.description:
When this option is configured, an authentication failure with the first server in the group causes the device to attempt authentication
with the next server in the list. The device attempts authentication with each server in the ordered list until either there is a successful
authentication or the list of servers in the group is exhausted.

load-balance.description: Enable load-balancing of authentication requests among different servers in a server group.

name.description: The name of the Server Group.For CX, maximum length is 32 characters.

servers.description: Configure the servers in the server group in a specific order based on their position within the group.

match-string.description: Substring to match in the auth string or the FQDN string (maximum 63 characters).

match-type.description:
Match authentication type for server rules in server-group. When the match-type is FQDN, the operator has to be NONE.
When the match-type is AUTHSTRING, the operator can be one of the 3 only: CONTAINS, EQUALS or STARTS-WITH.

x-enumDescriptions.FQDN: Match type to a FQDN string.

match-type.x-typeDescription: Match type for server rules in server-group.

operator.description:
Match authentication string to select next auth-server. When the match-type is AUTHSTRING, the operator can be one of the 3 only: CONTAINS, EQUALS or STARTS-WITH.
When the match-type is FQDN, the operator has to be NONE.

x-enumDescriptions.CONTAINS: Match when authstring contains the sub-string.
x-enumDescriptions.EQUALS: Match when authstring is equal to the sub-string
x-enumDescriptions.STARTS_WITH: Match when authstring starts-with the sub-string

operator.x-typeDescription: Match authentication string to select next auth-server

operator-match-string-match-type.description: Key combination of match-type,operator and match-string separated by ~

position.description: Position of auth-server, where default is bottom, 1 is top.

server-name.description:
The name of the Server to be associated to the Server Group.
For CX, maximum of 4 servers can be associated to a RADIUS Server Group and maximum of 1 server can be associated to a TACACS Server Group.

trim-fqdn.description: Trim FQDN from username before sending to server.

type.description: Auth Server Group Type. CX supports server group types RADIUS and TACACS and configuring this parameter is mandatory while creating a Server Group.

fail-through.description:
When this option is configured, an authentication failure with the first server in the group causes the device to attempt authentication
with the next server in the list. The device attempts authentication with each server in the ordered list until either there is a successful
authentication or the list of servers in the group is exhausted.

load-balance.description: Enable load-balancing of authentication requests among different servers in a server group.

servers.description: Configure the servers in the server group in a specific order based on their position within the group.

ArubaAuthServer_AuthServersSchemaGET.description:
Use this API to configure an external RADIUS server for user authentication. You can configure parameters such as server IP, authentication port, accounting port,
shared key, etc. This feature is applicable for all devices.

acct-modifier.description:
Attributes modifier for accounting-request.
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

acct-port.description:
Port number for accounting requests.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.
acct-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

admin-dn.description:
DN for the admin user who has read or search privileges across all of the entries in the LDAP database (the user does not need
write privileges but should be able to search the database and read attributes of other users in the database).
This field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

admin-password.description:
Password for the admin user.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.
admin-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

allow-cleartext.description:
Allow unencrypted communication with LDAP server.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

auth-modifier.description:
Attributes modifier for authentication-request.
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

auth-port.description:
Port number for authentication requests.For CX, default auth-port for RADIUS Servers is 1812 and TACACS Servers is 49.
For CX and PVOS, this parameter cannot be modified.
auth-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

auth-server-address.description: IP address or FQDN of the Auth server. For CX and PVOS, auth-server-address is a mandatory parameter and can not be modified.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

auth-throttle.description: AP measures request-response times for each packet exchange. As the time taken approaches the timeout, AP throttles new transactions.

authentication-type.description:
Specifies authentication protocol which is used for communication with Auth servers.
If not specified, the global timeout value will be picked. This field can be configured only when type is TACACS OR RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

base-dn.description:
DN name of the node which contains the entire user database to use.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

chase-referrals.description:
LDAP Chase Referrals.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

cppm-password-config.description:
Auth server Clearpass password configuration used for authentication.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

cppm-user-name.description:
User name of the ClearPass server to authenticate.
For CX, supported length is 2-64.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

deadtime.description:
Configures a dead time interval for the authentication server. When two or more authentication servers are configured on the Instant AP and
a server is unavailable, the dead time configuratidetermines the duration for which the authentication server would be available if the server
is marked as unavailable. the default value is 5min. time range is 1-1440.

default-authenticated-role.description:
Assign default role for users after XML server
 authorization.

domain.description:
Windows domain name.
this field can be configured only when type is WINDOWS.

gateway.description: Gateway IP address of the DRP VLAN

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

ip.description: IP address to be used as source IP for RADIUS packets and subnet mask of the DRP IP address.
ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

vlan.description: VLAN in which the RADIUS packets are sent.

dynamic-authorization-port.description: Configures the port number for sending AirGroup CoA, instead of the standard CoA port. The default value is 5999.
dynamic-authorization-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

enable.description: Enable Auth Server.

enable-radsec.description:
Enables RadSec for RADIUS data transport over TCP and TLS
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.
For CX and PVOS, this parameter cannot be modified.

enable-radsec-pcap.description:
Enables RadSec packet capture
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS.

event-timestamp-required.description:
Discard DAC request, if Event-Timestamp is not present
in DAC(Dynamic Authorization Client) request. This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as COA_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

filter.description:
Filter that should be applied to search of the user in the LDAP database.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP

ipsec-ah.description:
RADIUS server IPsec authentication parameters.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

ipsec-esp.description:
RADIUS server IPsec encryption parameters.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

encryption-key.description:
The plaintext key for IPsec encryption.
This field can be configured only when encryption-key-type is PLAIN_TEXT.

encryption-key-cipher-text.description:
The ciphertext key for IPsec encryption.
This field can be configured only when encryption-key-type is CIPHER_TEXT.

encryption-key-hexstring.description:
The hex-string key for IPsec encryption.
This field can be configured only when encryption-key-type is HEX_STRING.

encryption-key-type.description: The key type for IPsec encryption.

x-enumDescriptions.CIPHER_TEXT: Configure ciphertext type of IPsec key
x-enumDescriptions.HEX_STRING: Configure hex-string type of IPsec key
x-enumDescriptions.PLAIN_TEXT: Configure plaintext type of IPsec key

encryption-key-type.x-typeDescription: The key type for the IPsec authentication or the IPsec encryption.

encryption-type.description: Specify the algorithm to use for IPsec encryption: aes, des or 3des.

key-attribute.description:
The attribute that should be used as a key in search
for the LDAP server. This field can be configured only when type is LDAP

lower-case-mac-address.description:
Lower case MAC address.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

mac-address-delimiter.description:
MAC address delimiter.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

management-interface.description: Out of Band management interface for the Auth server.

max-connection.description:
Maximum number of simultaneous non-admin connections to a LDAP server.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP

max-retransmit-attempts.description:
Maximum number of retries sent to the server by the manager device(AP, GW and SW_CX) before the server is marked as down.
For AP, the default value is 3.
For CX, range is 0-5. If not specified, the global retry value will be picked.

msg-auth-required.description:
Enforcement of RADIUS message authenticator. if AP receives Access-Request/Access-Reject/Access-Challenge/CoA-Request/Disconnect-Request without message-authenticator present, the packet will be discarded.
For AP, this configuration is supported from 10.7.1.0.
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS

name.description: The name of the Auth Server.

nas-identifier.description:
NAS identifier to use in RADIUS packets.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

nas-ip-address.description: The NAS IP address to be sent in RADIUS packets from that server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

nas-ipv6-address.description: The NAS IPv6 address to be sent in RADIUS packets from that server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

port-access-keepalive.description:
Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active. Default value is status-server.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER:
Status-server messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE:
TCP keep alive messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active

port-access-keepalive.x-typeDescription: Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active

preferred-conn-type.description:
Preferred Connection Type.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

x-enumDescriptions.CLEAR_TEXT: set preferred connection type as Clear-Text
x-enumDescriptions.LDAP_S: set preferred connection type as LDAP-S
x-enumDescriptions.START_TLS: set preferred connection type as START-TLS

preferred-conn-type.x-typeDescription: Include VLAN identifier of port in acce7ss-request

radius-server-mode.description:
Specifies the mode in which the RADIUS server is used.
- AUTH_ONLY: Server is used only for authentication.
- COA_ONLY: Server is used only for Change of Authorization (CoA).
- AUTH_AND_COA: Server is used for both authentication and CoA.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_AND_COA: RADIUS server is used for both authentication and CoA.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_ONLY: RADIUS server is used only for authentication.
x-enumDescriptions.COA_ONLY: RADIUS server is used only for Change of Authorization (CoA).

radsec-client-cert.description:
RADIUS Security Client Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

radsec-est-cert-profile.description:
RADIUS Security Server Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

radsec-keepalive.description: If there is no radius activity for more than 15 minutes, with status-server keepalive, CPPM doesn't terminate connection every 15 minutes, with tcp-keepalive , CPPM terminates the RADSEC connection after 15 minutes

x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER:
Status-server messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE:
TCP keep alive messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active

radsec-keepalive.x-typeDescription: Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active

radsec-port.description:
Designates a RadSec port for RADIUS data transport
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.
For CX and PVOS, this parameter cannot be modified.
radsec-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

radsec-trusted-cacert-name.description:
RADIUS Security Trusted CA Cert Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

radsec-trusted-servercert-name.description:
RADIUS Security Server Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

replay-protection.description:
Replay protection for DAC(Dynamic Authorization Client) requests
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS and radius-server-mode is equal to COA_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

rfc5997.description:
When enabled, this parameter allows the Instant AP to send a status-server request to determine the actual status of the authentication or accounting server. This proves useful when there is a authentication or request time
rfc5997—RFC5997 support enabled for both authentication and accounting on the authentication server.
auth-only—RFC5997 support enabled for authentication only.
acct-only—RFC5997 support enabled for accounting only
no rfc5997—Disables RFC5997 support for the authentication server.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCT_ONLY: Query Status of Acct RADIUS Servers.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_ONLY: Query Status of Auth RADIUS Servers.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Query Status of auth and accounting RADIUS Servers.

rfc5997.x-typeDescription: Query Status of auth and accounting RADIUS Servers.

service-type.description:
Change Service Type default value to framed.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

x-enumDescriptions.FRAMED: Change Service-Type default value to framed for CP and MAC auth.
x-enumDescriptions.STANDARD: Follow RFC, the default Service-Type for 802.1x is framed, for CP is Login, for mac is Call-Back.

service-type.x-typeDescription: Change the default value of service type.

service-type-frame-1x.description: Change Service-Type value to frame for 802.1x.

service-type-login-1x.description: Change Service-Type value to login for 802.1x.

session-authorization.description:
Enable TACACS+ authorization.
this field can be configured only when type is TACACS.

shared-secret-config.description: This passkey is used as shared-secret for encrypting the communication between Auth server and device.

source-address.description: Source IP address to use in messages to the Auth server

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

source-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN Interface to use in messages to the Auth server
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS or RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.
source-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tcp-port.description:
Port number used by the server.
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS
tcp-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

timeout.description:
Maximum time, in seconds, that the manager device(AP, GW and SW_CX) waits before timing out the request and resending it.
For CX, range is 1-60. If not specified, the global timeout value will be picked.
For AP, the default value of RADIUS and LDAP is 5, TACACS is 20.
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS, LDAP, or RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

tls-initial-connection-timeout.description:
TLS Initial connection timeout (timeout for TLS handshake).
For CX, if not specified, the global TLS Initial connection timeout value will be picked.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

tracking-enable.description:
Enable server tracking.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA or TACACS

tracking-method.description:
Tracking method of the server. Default value for tracking method is access-request.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCESS_REQUEST: Track the server using access-request messages
x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER: Track the server using status-server messages
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE: Track the server using TCP keep-alive messages

tracking-method.x-typeDescription: Tracking method type of the server

tracking-mode.description:
Tracking mode of the server. Default value of tracking-mode is any.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

x-enumDescriptions.ANY: Track the server irrespective of its availability.
x-enumDescriptions.DEAD_ONLY: Track the server only if it is not reachable.

tracking-mode.x-typeDescription: Tracking mode type of the server

type.description:
Auth Server Type. AP support server types are RADIUS, LDAP, TACACS and XMLAPI.
CX supports server types RADIUS and TACACS. PVOS supports RADIUS and TACACS.
Type is a mandatory parameter for CX and PVOS and can not be modified.
GW supports RADIUS, TACACS, WINDOWS and LDAP

use-ip-for-calling-station-id.description:
Use IP for Calling Station Id.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

use-md5.description:
Use MD5 hash for cleartext password.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

vrf.description:
VRF for the Auth server.
For CX, this parameter cannot be modified.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

x-enumDescriptions.PLUS_OR_MINUS: Sets the acceptable time-window value as PLUS OR MINUS
x-enumDescriptions.POSITIVE: Sets the acceptable time-window value as default PLUS

window-type.x-typeDescription: Time-window value type for dynamic authorization messages.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

xmlapi-server-key.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

acct-modifier.description:
Attributes modifier for accounting-request.
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

acct-port.description:
Port number for accounting requests.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.
acct-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

admin-dn.description:
DN for the admin user who has read or search privileges across all of the entries in the LDAP database (the user does not need
write privileges but should be able to search the database and read attributes of other users in the database).
This field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

admin-password.description:
Password for the admin user.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.
admin-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

allow-cleartext.description:
Allow unencrypted communication with LDAP server.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

auth-modifier.description:
Attributes modifier for authentication-request.
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

auth-port.description:
Port number for authentication requests.For CX, default auth-port for RADIUS Servers is 1812 and TACACS Servers is 49.
For CX and PVOS, this parameter cannot be modified.
auth-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

auth-server-address.description: IP address or FQDN of the Auth server. For CX and PVOS, auth-server-address is a mandatory parameter and can not be modified.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

auth-throttle.description: AP measures request-response times for each packet exchange. As the time taken approaches the timeout, AP throttles new transactions.

authentication-type.description:
Specifies authentication protocol which is used for communication with Auth servers.
If not specified, the global timeout value will be picked. This field can be configured only when type is TACACS OR RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

base-dn.description:
DN name of the node which contains the entire user database to use.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

chase-referrals.description:
LDAP Chase Referrals.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

cppm-password-config.description:
Auth server Clearpass password configuration used for authentication.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

cppm-user-name.description:
User name of the ClearPass server to authenticate.
For CX, supported length is 2-64.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

deadtime.description:
Configures a dead time interval for the authentication server. When two or more authentication servers are configured on the Instant AP and
a server is unavailable, the dead time configuratidetermines the duration for which the authentication server would be available if the server
is marked as unavailable. the default value is 5min. time range is 1-1440.

default-authenticated-role.description:
Assign default role for users after XML server
 authorization.

domain.description:
Windows domain name.
this field can be configured only when type is WINDOWS.

gateway.description: Gateway IP address of the DRP VLAN

An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

vlan.description: VLAN in which the RADIUS packets are sent.

dynamic-authorization-port.description: Configures the port number for sending AirGroup CoA, instead of the standard CoA port. The default value is 5999.
dynamic-authorization-port.x-typeDescription:

or UDP.

enable.description: Enable Auth Server.

enable-radsec-pcap.description:
