filter.description:
Filter that should be applied to search of the user in the LDAP database.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP

ipsec-ah.description:
RADIUS server IPsec authentication parameters.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

ipsec-esp.description:
RADIUS server IPsec encryption parameters.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

encryption-key.description:
The plaintext key for IPsec encryption.
This field can be configured only when encryption-key-type is PLAIN_TEXT.

encryption-key-cipher-text.description:
The ciphertext key for IPsec encryption.
This field can be configured only when encryption-key-type is CIPHER_TEXT.

encryption-key-hexstring.description:
The hex-string key for IPsec encryption.
This field can be configured only when encryption-key-type is HEX_STRING.

encryption-key-type.description: The key type for IPsec encryption.

x-enumDescriptions.CIPHER_TEXT: Configure ciphertext type of IPsec key
x-enumDescriptions.HEX_STRING: Configure hex-string type of IPsec key
x-enumDescriptions.PLAIN_TEXT: Configure plaintext type of IPsec key

encryption-key-type.x-typeDescription: The key type for the IPsec authentication or the IPsec encryption.

encryption-type.description: Specify the algorithm to use for IPsec encryption: aes, des or 3des.

key-attribute.description:
The attribute that should be used as a key in search
for the LDAP server. This field can be configured only when type is LDAP

lower-case-mac-address.description:
Lower case MAC address.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

mac-address-delimiter.description:
MAC address delimiter.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

management-interface.description: Out of Band management interface for the Auth server.

max-connection.description:
Maximum number of simultaneous non-admin connections to a LDAP server.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP

max-retransmit-attempts.description:
Maximum number of retries sent to the server by the manager device(AP, GW and SW_CX) before the server is marked as down.
For AP, the default value is 3.
For CX, range is 0-5. If not specified, the global retry value will be picked.

msg-auth-required.description:
Enforcement of RADIUS message authenticator. if AP receives Access-Request/Access-Reject/Access-Challenge/CoA-Request/Disconnect-Request without message-authenticator present, the packet will be discarded.
For AP, this configuration is supported from 10.7.1.0.
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS

name.description: The name of the Auth Server.

nas-identifier.description:
NAS identifier to use in RADIUS packets.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

nas-ip-address.description: The NAS IP address to be sent in RADIUS packets from that server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

nas-ipv6-address.description: The NAS IPv6 address to be sent in RADIUS packets from that server.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

port-access-keepalive.description:
Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active. Default value is status-server.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER:
Status-server messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE:
TCP keep alive messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active

port-access-keepalive.x-typeDescription: Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active

preferred-conn-type.description:
Preferred Connection Type.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

x-enumDescriptions.CLEAR_TEXT: set preferred connection type as Clear-Text
x-enumDescriptions.LDAP_S: set preferred connection type as LDAP-S
x-enumDescriptions.START_TLS: set preferred connection type as START-TLS

preferred-conn-type.x-typeDescription: Include VLAN identifier of port in acce7ss-request

radius-server-mode.description:
Specifies the mode in which the RADIUS server is used.
- AUTH_ONLY: Server is used only for authentication.
- COA_ONLY: Server is used only for Change of Authorization (CoA).
- AUTH_AND_COA: Server is used for both authentication and CoA.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_AND_COA: RADIUS server is used for both authentication and CoA.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_ONLY: RADIUS server is used only for authentication.
x-enumDescriptions.COA_ONLY: RADIUS server is used only for Change of Authorization (CoA).

radsec-client-cert.description:
RADIUS Security Client Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

radsec-est-cert-profile.description:
RADIUS Security Server Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

radsec-keepalive.description: If there is no radius activity for more than 15 minutes, with status-server keepalive, CPPM doesn't terminate connection every 15 minutes, with tcp-keepalive , CPPM terminates the RADSEC connection after 15 minutes

x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER:
Status-server messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE:
TCP keep alive messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active

radsec-keepalive.x-typeDescription: Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active

radsec-port.description:
Designates a RadSec port for RADIUS data transport
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.
For CX and PVOS, this parameter cannot be modified.
radsec-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

radsec-trusted-cacert-name.description:
RADIUS Security Trusted CA Cert Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

radsec-trusted-servercert-name.description:
RADIUS Security Server Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

replay-protection.description:
Replay protection for DAC(Dynamic Authorization Client) requests
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS and radius-server-mode is equal to COA_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

rfc5997.description:
When enabled, this parameter allows the Instant AP to send a status-server request to determine the actual status of the authentication or accounting server. This proves useful when there is a authentication or request time
rfc5997—RFC5997 support enabled for both authentication and accounting on the authentication server.
auth-only—RFC5997 support enabled for authentication only.
acct-only—RFC5997 support enabled for accounting only
no rfc5997—Disables RFC5997 support for the authentication server.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCT_ONLY: Query Status of Acct RADIUS Servers.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_ONLY: Query Status of Auth RADIUS Servers.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Query Status of auth and accounting RADIUS Servers.

rfc5997.x-typeDescription: Query Status of auth and accounting RADIUS Servers.

service-type.description:
Change Service Type default value to framed.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

x-enumDescriptions.FRAMED: Change Service-Type default value to framed for CP and MAC auth.
x-enumDescriptions.STANDARD: Follow RFC, the default Service-Type for 802.1x is framed, for CP is Login, for mac is Call-Back.

service-type.x-typeDescription: Change the default value of service type.

service-type-frame-1x.description: Change Service-Type value to frame for 802.1x.

service-type-login-1x.description: Change Service-Type value to login for 802.1x.

session-authorization.description:
Enable TACACS+ authorization.
this field can be configured only when type is TACACS.

shared-secret-config.description: This passkey is used as shared-secret for encrypting the communication between Auth server and device.

source-address.description: Source IP address to use in messages to the Auth server

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

source-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN Interface to use in messages to the Auth server
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS or RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.
source-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tcp-port.description:
Port number used by the server.
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS
tcp-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

timeout.description:
Maximum time, in seconds, that the manager device(AP, GW and SW_CX) waits before timing out the request and resending it.
For CX, range is 1-60. If not specified, the global timeout value will be picked.
For AP, the default value of RADIUS and LDAP is 5, TACACS is 20.
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS, LDAP, or RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

tls-initial-connection-timeout.description:
TLS Initial connection timeout (timeout for TLS handshake).
For CX, if not specified, the global TLS Initial connection timeout value will be picked.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

tracking-enable.description:
Enable server tracking.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA or TACACS

tracking-method.description:
Tracking method of the server. Default value for tracking method is access-request.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCESS_REQUEST: Track the server using access-request messages
x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER: Track the server using status-server messages
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE: Track the server using TCP keep-alive messages

tracking-method.x-typeDescription: Tracking method type of the server

tracking-mode.description:
Tracking mode of the server. Default value of tracking-mode is any.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

x-enumDescriptions.ANY: Track the server irrespective of its availability.
x-enumDescriptions.DEAD_ONLY: Track the server only if it is not reachable.

tracking-mode.x-typeDescription: Tracking mode type of the server

type.description:
Auth Server Type. AP support server types are RADIUS, LDAP, TACACS and XMLAPI.
CX supports server types RADIUS and TACACS. PVOS supports RADIUS and TACACS.
Type is a mandatory parameter for CX and PVOS and can not be modified.
GW supports RADIUS, TACACS, WINDOWS and LDAP

use-ip-for-calling-station-id.description:
Use IP for Calling Station Id.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

use-md5.description:
Use MD5 hash for cleartext password.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

vrf.description:
VRF for the Auth server.
For CX, this parameter cannot be modified.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

x-enumDescriptions.PLUS_OR_MINUS: Sets the acceptable time-window value as PLUS OR MINUS
x-enumDescriptions.POSITIVE: Sets the acceptable time-window value as default PLUS

window-type.x-typeDescription: Time-window value type for dynamic authorization messages.

a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE:
TCP keep alive messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active

port-access-keepalive.x-typeDescription: Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active

preferred-conn-type.description:
Preferred Connection Type.
this field can be configured only when type is LDAP.

x-enumDescriptions.CLEAR_TEXT: set preferred connection type as Clear-Text
x-enumDescriptions.LDAP_S: set preferred connection type as LDAP-S
x-enumDescriptions.START_TLS: set preferred connection type as START-TLS

preferred-conn-type.x-typeDescription: Include VLAN identifier of port in acce7ss-request

radius-server-mode.description:
Specifies the mode in which the RADIUS server is used.
- AUTH_ONLY: Server is used only for authentication.
- COA_ONLY: Server is used only for Change of Authorization (CoA).
- AUTH_AND_COA: Server is used for both authentication and CoA.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_AND_COA: RADIUS server is used for both authentication and CoA.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_ONLY: RADIUS server is used only for authentication.
x-enumDescriptions.COA_ONLY: RADIUS server is used only for Change of Authorization (CoA).

radsec-client-cert.description:
RADIUS Security Client Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

radsec-est-cert-profile.description:
RADIUS Security Server Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

radsec-keepalive.description: If there is no radius activity for more than 15 minutes, with status-server keepalive, CPPM doesn't terminate connection every 15 minutes, with tcp-keepalive , CPPM terminates the RADSEC connection after 15 minutes

x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER:
Status-server messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE:
TCP keep alive messages are used for keeping
RadSec port-access tunnel active

radsec-keepalive.x-typeDescription: Method to keep RadSec port-access tunnel active

radsec-port.description:
Designates a RadSec port for RADIUS data transport
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.
For CX and PVOS, this parameter cannot be modified.
radsec-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

radsec-trusted-cacert-name.description:
RADIUS Security Trusted CA Cert Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

radsec-trusted-servercert-name.description:
RADIUS Security Server Certificate Name.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

replay-protection.description:
Replay protection for DAC(Dynamic Authorization Client) requests
this field can be configured only when type is RADIUS and radius-server-mode is equal to COA_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

rfc5997.description:
When enabled, this parameter allows the Instant AP to send a status-server request to determine the actual status of the authentication or accounting server. This proves useful when there is a authentication or request time
rfc5997—RFC5997 support enabled for both authentication and accounting on the authentication server.
auth-only—RFC5997 support enabled for authentication only.
acct-only—RFC5997 support enabled for accounting only
no rfc5997—Disables RFC5997 support for the authentication server.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCT_ONLY: Query Status of Acct RADIUS Servers.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTH_ONLY: Query Status of Auth RADIUS Servers.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Query Status of auth and accounting RADIUS Servers.

rfc5997.x-typeDescription: Query Status of auth and accounting RADIUS Servers.

service-type.description:
Change Service Type default value to framed.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

x-enumDescriptions.FRAMED: Change Service-Type default value to framed for CP and MAC auth.
x-enumDescriptions.STANDARD: Follow RFC, the default Service-Type for 802.1x is framed, for CP is Login, for mac is Call-Back.

service-type.x-typeDescription: Change the default value of service type.

service-type-frame-1x.description: Change Service-Type value to frame for 802.1x.

service-type-login-1x.description: Change Service-Type value to login for 802.1x.

session-authorization.description:
Enable TACACS+ authorization.
this field can be configured only when type is TACACS.

shared-secret-config.description: This passkey is used as shared-secret for encrypting the communication between Auth server and device.

source-address.description: Source IP address to use in messages to the Auth server

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

source-interface-vlan.description:
Source VLAN Interface to use in messages to the Auth server
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS or RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.
source-interface-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tcp-port.description:
Port number used by the server.
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS
tcp-port.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

timeout.description:
Maximum time, in seconds, that the manager device(AP, GW and SW_CX) waits before timing out the request and resending it.
For CX, range is 1-60. If not specified, the global timeout value will be picked.
For AP, the default value of RADIUS and LDAP is 5, TACACS is 20.
This field can be configured only when type is TACACS, LDAP, or RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

tls-initial-connection-timeout.description:
TLS Initial connection timeout (timeout for TLS handshake).
For CX, if not specified, the global TLS Initial connection timeout value will be picked.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

tracking-enable.description:
Enable server tracking.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA or TACACS

tracking-method.description:
Tracking method of the server. Default value for tracking method is access-request.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA and enable-radsec is set to true.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCESS_REQUEST: Track the server using access-request messages
x-enumDescriptions.STATUS_SERVER: Track the server using status-server messages
x-enumDescriptions.TCP_KEEPALIVE: Track the server using TCP keep-alive messages

tracking-method.x-typeDescription: Tracking method type of the server

tracking-mode.description:
Tracking mode of the server. Default value of tracking-mode is any.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA

x-enumDescriptions.ANY: Track the server irrespective of its availability.
x-enumDescriptions.DEAD_ONLY: Track the server only if it is not reachable.

tracking-mode.x-typeDescription: Tracking mode type of the server

type.description:
Auth Server Type. AP support server types are RADIUS, LDAP, TACACS and XMLAPI.
CX supports server types RADIUS and TACACS. PVOS supports RADIUS and TACACS.
Type is a mandatory parameter for CX and PVOS and can not be modified.
GW supports RADIUS, TACACS, WINDOWS and LDAP

use-ip-for-calling-station-id.description:
Use IP for Calling Station Id.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

use-md5.description:
Use MD5 hash for cleartext password.
This field can be configured only when type is RADIUS with radius-server-mode configured as AUTH_ONLY or AUTH_AND_COA.

vrf.description:
VRF for the Auth server.
For CX, this parameter cannot be modified.
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

x-enumDescriptions.PLUS_OR_MINUS: Sets the acceptable time-window value as PLUS OR MINUS
x-enumDescriptions.POSITIVE: Sets the acceptable time-window value as default PLUS

window-type.x-typeDescription: Time-window value type for dynamic authorization messages.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

xmlapi-server-key.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

called-station-id.description:
Configure this parameter to be sent with the RADIUS attribute Called Station ID for authentication and accounting requests. For AP, this API is applicable for
WLAN profile and AP port profile. For GW, this API is applicable for the authentication server profile. This feature is applicable for AP and GW.

include-ssid-delimiter.description: The delimiter between mac address and SSID. For example, the delimiter is dash. The called-station-id like aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff-wlanssid.

mac-address-delimiter.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string and no delimiter, when configure mac-address-delimiter, AP MAC Address will be added delimiter in authentication packet.

type.description:
Configure the called-station-id types.
For AP, The type includes AP group, AP MAC address, AP name, AP IP or user vlan. The default value is AP MAC address.

upper-case.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string, when enabled upper-case, AP MAC Address will be changed to upper case in authentication packet.

called-station-id.description:
Configure this parameter to be sent with the RADIUS attribute Called Station ID for authentication and accounting requests. For AP, this API is applicable for
WLAN profile and AP port profile. For GW, this API is applicable for the authentication server profile. This feature is applicable for AP and GW.

include-ssid-delimiter.description: The delimiter between mac address and SSID. For example, the delimiter is dash. The called-station-id like aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff-wlanssid.

mac-address-delimiter.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string and no delimiter, when configure mac-address-delimiter, AP MAC Address will be added delimiter in authentication packet.

type.description:
Configure the called-station-id types.
For AP, The type includes AP group, AP MAC address, AP name, AP IP or user vlan. The default value is AP MAC address.

upper-case.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string, when enabled upper-case, AP MAC Address will be changed to upper case in authentication packet.

ArubaAuthSurvivability_AuthSurvivability.description:
Configure the paramaters of authentication survivability. The authentication survivability feature requires ClearPass Policy Manager 6.0.2 or later,
and is applicable only when external servers such as RADIUS are configured for the SSID. When enabled, AP authenticates the previously connected clients using
EAP-TLS or MAC authentication even when connectivity to ClearPass Policy Manager is temporarily lost. The authentication survivability feature is not applicable when
a RADIUS server is configured as an internal server. This feature is only applicable for GW.

profile.description: Configure the paramaters of authentication survivability.

ca-cert.description: Client's CA Certificate for Survival Server.

cache-lifetime.description:
This parameter specifies the lifetime in hours for the cached access credential in the local Survival Server. When the specified cache-lifetime expires, the cached access credential is deleted from the managed device.
The default value is 24h, the time range is 1-168 for GW.

enable.description: This parameter controls whether to use the Survival Server when no other servers are in-service.

ca-cert.description: Client's CA Certificate for Survival Server.

cache-lifetime.description:
This parameter specifies the lifetime in hours for the cached access credential in the local Survival Server. When the specified cache-lifetime expires, the cached access credential is deleted from the managed device.
The default value is 24h, the time range is 1-168 for GW.

enable.description: This parameter controls whether to use the Survival Server when no other servers are in-service.

ca-cert.description: Client's CA Certificate for Survival Server.

cache-lifetime.description:
This parameter specifies the lifetime in hours for the cached access credential in the local Survival Server. When the specified cache-lifetime expires, the cached access credential is deleted from the managed device.
The default value is 24h, the time range is 1-168 for GW.

enable.description: This parameter controls whether to use the Survival Server when no other servers are in-service.

subject.description: The entity a certificate belongs to: a machine, an individual, or an organization.

valid-from.description: Starting date of validity for the certificate.
valid-from.x-typeDescription:
A date and time, expressed in the format described in RFC3339.
That is to say:

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ+-hh:mm

where YYYY is the year, MM is the month expressed as a two-digit
month (zero padding if required), DD is the day of the month,
expressed as a two digit value. T is the literal character 'T',
HH is the hour of the day expressed as a two digit number, using
the 24-hour clock, MM is the minute of the hour expressed as a
two digit number. Z is the literal character 'Z', followed by a
timezone offset expressed in hours (hh) and minutes (mm), both
expressed as two digit numbers. The time offset is specified as
a positive or negative offset to UTC using the '+' or '-'
character preceding the offset.

Optionally, fractional seconds can be expressed after the minute
of the hour as a decimal number of unspecified precision
reflecting fractions of a second.

valid-to.x-typeDescription:
A date and time, expressed in the format described in RFC3339.
That is to say:

YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ+-hh:mm

where YYYY is the year, MM is the month expressed as a two-digit
month (zero padding if required), DD is the day of the month,
expressed as a two digit value. T is the literal character 'T',
HH is the hour of the day expressed as a two digit number, using
the 24-hour clock, MM is the minute of the hour expressed as a
two digit number. Z is the literal character 'Z', followed by a
timezone offset expressed in hours (hh) and minutes (mm), both
expressed as two digit numbers. The time offset is specified as
a positive or negative offset to UTC using the '+' or '-'
character preceding the offset.

Optionally, fractional seconds can be expressed after the minute
of the hour as a decimal number of unspecified precision
reflecting fractions of a second.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
The ipv4-address type represents an IPv4 address in
dotted-quad notation.  The IPv4 address may include a zone
index, separated by a % sign.
The zone index is used to disambiguate identical address
values.  For link-local addresses, the zone index will
typically be the interface index number or the name of an
interface.  If the zone index is not present, the default
zone of the device will be used.
The canonical format for the zone index is the numerical
format

The ipv6-address type represents an IPv6 address in full,
mixed, shortened, and shortened-mixed notation.  The IPv6
address may include a zone index, separated by a % sign.
The zone index is used to disambiguate identical address
values.  For link-local addresses, the zone index will
typically be the interface index number or the name of an
interface.  If the zone index is not present, the default
zone of the device will be used.
The canonical format of IPv6 addresses uses the textual
representation defined in Section 4 of RFC 5952.  The
canonical format for the zone index is the numerical
format as described in Section 11.2 of RFC 4007.

assignment-rules.description:
Assign the client a user role, VLAN ID or VLAN name based on attributes. For GW and AP, the attributes is returned for the client by the authentication server and this API is applicable for
auth server group. For AP, this API is also applicable WLAN profile and if this API works in WLAN profile, the attributes can be returned for the client by the authentication server or other
attributes such as mac-address, dhcp-option, AP-Name, etc. Rules are ordered: the first rule that matches the configured condition is applied. VLAN IDs and VLAN names cannot be listed together.
This feature is only applicable for AP and GW.

attribute.description:
Attribute returned by the authentication server.
For AP, it also includes some custom attributes like mac-address, dhcp-option and fw_mode.

named-vlan.description: This is the assigned named VLAN when there is a rule match.

operand.description:
Match operand, For GW, the VSA value. For AP, it is VSA value under server group profile.
It is VSA value and mac-addrees, dhcp-option and fw_mode under SSID profile and AP wired port profile.

role.description: This is the assigned Role when there is a rule match.

sequence-id.description: The sequence id determines the rule order.

vlan.description: This is the assigned VLAN when there is a rule match.

ArubaEst_EstProfilesSchemaGET.description:
Configurate the parameters of Enrollment over Secure Transport(EST) Profile. EST supports automatic enrollment of certificates with the EST Server. The certificates can be enrolled or re-enrolled automatically by
configuring an EST profile on the device. Certificate Enrollment with EST allows users to use their own PKI instead of the factory or self-signed certificates available on the Instant AP. This enables the user to
have maximum visibility and control over the management of the PKI used and address any issues related to security by themselves in a scaled environment.This feature is applicable for all devices.

profile.description:
Configurate the parameters of Enrollment over Secure Transport(EST) Profile. You can configure parameters such as server-host, server-port,
arbitrary-label, etc.
For AP, one EST profile is supported for per device.
For CX device, maximum of 16 profiles supported.

arbitrary-label.description:
Arbitrary Label used in EST enrollment/reenrollment operations.
For AP device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For CX device, valid length: 1-64 characters.

arbitrary-label-enrollment.description:
Arbitrary Label used in EST enrollment.
For Gateway device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For AP device, valid length: 1-63 characters.

arbitrary-label-reenrollment.description:
Arbitrary Label used in EST reenrollment.
For Gateway device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For AP device, valid length: 1-63 characters.

challenge-password.description:
Challenge Password used in EST enrollment. This configuration is valid only if
username is not configured.
challenge-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

csr-attribute.description:
CSR Attribute used in EST enrollment.
Following values are valid for AP and Gateway devices:
1. RSA_SHA256: RSA-2048 with SHA256 digest as CSR Attr value.
2. ECDSA_PRIME256V1_SHA256: ECDSA NIST P-256 with SHA256 digest as CSR Attr value
3. ECDSA_PRIME384R1_SHA384: ECDSA NIST P-384 with SHA384 digest as CSR Attr value
Below values are supported only for AP devices:
1. RSA_SHA384: RSA with SHA384 as CSR Attr value
2. RSA_SHA512: RSA with SHA512 as CSR Attr value

x-enumDescriptions.ECDSA_PRIME256V1_SHA256: ECDSA NIST P-256 with SHA256 digest as CSR Attr value.
x-enumDescriptions.ECDSA_PRIME384R1_SHA384: ECDSA NIST P-384 with SHA384 digest as CSR Attr value.
x-enumDescriptions.RSA_SHA256: RSA-2048 with SHA256 digest as CSR Attr value.
x-enumDescriptions.RSA_SHA384: RSA with SHA384 as CSR Attr value
x-enumDescriptions.RSA_SHA512: RSA with SHA512 as CSR Attr value

csr-attribute.x-typeDescription: CSR(Certificate Signing Request) Attr value type

enable.description: This configuration is used to activate or deactivate EST profile.

key-length.description: Length of RSA key used in CSR

name.description:
EST Profile name to be configured.
For AP device, valid length: 1-32 characters.
For CX device, valid length: 1-32 characters.

organizational-unit-name.description: Organization-Unit used in CSR attribute value.

password.description:
Password of the EST Client. This configuration is valid only if username is configured.
For Gateway device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For AP device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

reenrollment-prior-expiry.description: Number of days prior certificate expiry to initiate reenrollment

retry-interval.description: Retry-interval in seconds for EST operations

server-host.description:
EST Server's hostname/IP address.
For CX device, valid length: 1-162 characters.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

server-vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

x-enumDescriptions.RSA_SHA384: RSA with SHA384 as CSR Attr value
x-enumDescriptions.RSA_SHA512: RSA with SHA512 as CSR Attr value

csr-attribute.x-typeDescription: CSR(Certificate Signing Request) Attr value type

enable.description: This configuration is used to activate or deactivate EST profile.

key-length.description: Length of RSA key used in CSR

name.description:
EST Profile name to be configured.
For AP device, valid length: 1-32 characters.
For CX device, valid length: 1-32 characters.

organizational-unit-name.description: Organization-Unit used in CSR attribute value.

password.description:
Password of the EST Client. This configuration is valid only if username is configured.
For Gateway device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For AP device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

reenrollment-prior-expiry.description: Number of days prior certificate expiry to initiate reenrollment

retry-interval.description: Retry-interval in seconds for EST operations

server-host.description:
EST Server's hostname/IP address.
For CX device, valid length: 1-162 characters.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

server-vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

username.description:
Username of the EST Client.
For Gateway device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For AP device, valid length: 1-63 characters.
For CX device, valid length: 1-32 characters.

ArubaFirewall_FirewallSchemaGET.description:
Firewall monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic
based on predefined security rules. It protects against unauthorized
access, cyberattacks, and malicious threats. Firewall is superset of
several of its submodules which all together help in keeping traffic
secure. This model defines the Aruba firewall profile hierarchy used
by APs and Gateways to enforce stateful packet inspection and
policy-based traffic controls. It organizes firewall configuration
into reusable profiles with separate IPv4 and IPv6 subtrees, and
exposes tunables for session handling, traffic classification, rate
limiting/mitigation controls, multicast handling, and other datapath
behaviors.

profile.description:
A named firewall profile that encapsulates a complete set of IPv4 and
IPv6 firewall policies for a given deployment context. Only a single
firewall profile instance is permitted per scope. Each profile is
identified by a unique name and contains separate IPv4 and IPv6
containers with tunables for session management, traffic classification,
spoofing protection, multicast optimization, and various datapath behaviors.
Profiles can be created, updated, and deleted independently, allowing
administrators to maintain multiple policy sets and apply them to different
device groups or network segments as needed.

allow-tri-session.description: Allows three-way session when performing destination NAT. This option should be enabled when the managed device is not the default gateway for wireless clients and the default gateway is behind the managed device. This option is typically used for captive portal configuration.

amsdu.description: Aggregated Medium Access Control Service Data Units (AMSDU) packets are dropped if this option is enabled

attack-rate.description: List of attack-rate protocol, rate and action configurations. Sets rates which, if exceeded, can indicate a denial of service attack.

action.description: Action to take on violation of rate

x-enumDescriptions.DENYLIST: Add the session to the deny list.

protocol.description: Protocol to monitor for Denial of Service attacks.

x-enumDescriptions.ARP: Monitor/police ARP attack (non Gratuitous ARP). Default rate 100
x-enumDescriptions.GRAT_ARP: Monitor/police Gratuitous ARP attack. Default rate 50

protocol.x-typeDescription: Protocol to monitor for ipv4 Denial of Service attacks.

rate.description: Number of requests per 30 seconds. Valid range 1-16384

bwcontracts-subnet-broadcast.description: Apply bandwidth contracts to local subnet broadcast traffic

cp-bandwidth-contract.description: Configures a list of Control-Path bandwidth contract traffic rate limits, in packets per second, to prevent denial of service attacks.

protocol.description: Protocol type whose contract is being specified

x-enumDescriptions.AUTH: Rate limit traffic to the AUTH process
x-enumDescriptions.IKE: Rate limit IKE traffic to Control Plane
x-enumDescriptions.L2_OTHER: Rate limit L2 protocol and L2 special handling traffic
x-enumDescriptions.ROUTE: Rate limit traffic that needs ARP requests generated
x-enumDescriptions.SESSMIRR: Rate limit session mirrored traffic to Control Plane
x-enumDescriptions.VRRP: Rate limit VRRP traffic to Control Plane

deny-needfrag-df-gre.description: Drops IP packets with DF bit set when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is not sent.

deny-needfrag-df-gre-xmit-icmp.description: Drops IP packets with DF bit set when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is sent.

deny-needfrag-df-ipsec.description: Drops IP packets with DF bit set when packet length is greater than IPsec tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is not sent.

deny-needfrag-gre.description: Drops IP packets when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is not sent

deny-needfrag-gre-xmit-icmp.description: Drops IP packets when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is sent.

deny-source-routing.description: Disallow forwarding of IP frames with source routing options set

dhcp-perf-monitoring.description: Enables DHCP performance monitoring. This parameter is used to measure the time taken for a DHCP exchange.

disable-ftp-server.description: Enables DHCP performance monitoring. This parameter is used to measure the time taken for a DHCP exchange.Enabling this option could cause APs to not boot up. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. disabled

drop-ip-fragments.description: When enabled, all IP fragments are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-bridging.description: Enables bridging when the managed device is in factory default

enable-gre-inner-pkt-frag.description: Enables fragmenting inner IP frames when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU.

enable-per-packet-logging.description: Enables logging of every packet if logging is enabled for the corresponding session rule. Normally, one event is logged per session. If you enable this option, each packet in the session is logged. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative, as doing so may create unnecessary overhead on the managed device.

enable-port-packet-drop-logging.description: Enables port packet logging. If enabled, the dropped frames are logged. Disabled by default

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

gre-call-id-processing.description: Creates a unique state for each PPTP tunnel. Do not enable this option unless instructed to do so by a technical support representative.

immediate-feedback.description:
Triggers datapath immediate freeback of buffers.
Applicable to 72xx Platform only(Default disabled)

wired-server-ip.description: IP address of the wired server

items.description: IP address of the wired server
items.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

wired-server-ipv6.description: IPv6 address of the wired server

items.description: IPv6 address of the wired server
items.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

wired-server-mac.description: MAC address of the wired server

items.description: MAC address of the wired server

ip-classification.description:
Enable IP reputation/geolocation classification (Need
to configure IP name server)

local-valid-users.description: Only allow IP addresses of local subnets in user table

log-icmp-error.description: Logs received ICMP errors. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by a customer support representative. Disabled by default

maximum-threshold.description: Configures maximum threshold. Requires minimum-threshold to be configured. Range 0-100.

optimize-dad-frames.description:
Optimizes DAD(Duplicate Address Detection) frames on wireless - reduce flooding of
IPv4 gratuitous ARPs/IPv6 DAD frames onto wireless clients. Enabled by default

outstanding-buffers-hwm-percent.description: Outstanding Buffers High Water Mark Percentage(1-99)

prevent-dhcp-exhaustion.description: Enable check for DHCP client hardware address against the packet source MAC address. This command checks the frame's source-MAC against the DHCPv4 client hardware address and drops the packet if it does not match. Enabling this feature prevents a client from submitting multiple DHCP requests with different hardware addresses, thereby preventing DHCP pool depletion.

prohibit-arp-spoofing.description: Detects and prohibits arp spoofing. When this option is enabled, possible arp spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent.

prohibit-ip-spoof-all.description: Detects ARP spoofing, when an intruder sends ARP using the IP and MAC addresses of a known user. When this option is enabled, additional check for the user route cache entry is performed. If the route cache entry is not found or if found, but the route cache entry MAC does not match with the sender MAC in ARP frame, then, the frame is marked as spoof. The IP spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent. If the Prohibit IP Spoofing option is enabled, the controller denies all the traffic from the client using an IP address that is used by another client that has an entry in the user table. This option does not allow multiple MAC addresses to use the same IP address.

prohibit-ip-spoofing.description: Detects IP spoofing (where an intruder sends messages using the IP address of a trusted client). When this option is enabled, source and destination IP and MAC addresses are checked; possible IP spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent. Enabled in IPv4, disabled in IPv6.

prohibit-rc-update.description: This option prohibits route-cache update on user add. Disabled by default.

prohibit-rst-replay.description: Closes a TCP connection in both directions if a TCP RST is received from either direction. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Disabled by default

restrict-corp-access.description: Restricted access to the corporate network.

session-idle-timeout.description: Time, in seconds, that a non-TCP session can be idle before it is removed from the session table. You should not modify this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Default value is 16. Valid range 16-300

session-spread.description: Enables counter CPU to perform encryption. Applicable to 7200 Series controllers only Disabled by default

session-tunnel-fib.description:
Enable session,tunnel based forwarding. !!Warning!! It
is recommended to toggle this knob during maintenance
window or OFF peak production hours. On M3 this knob
will enable ONLY tunnel based forwarding, session based
does NOT apply to this platform.

shape-mcast.description: Enables multicast optimization and provides excellent streaming quality regardless of the amount of VLANs or IP IGMP groups that are used. Disabled by default.

stall-crash.description: Triggers datapath crash on stall detection. Applies to the to 7200 Series managed device only

tcp-session-idle-timeout.description: Time, in seconds, that a TCP session can be idle before it is removed from the session table. You should not modify this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Default value is 16. Valid range 300-43200

track-spoof.description: Detects spoof in datapath. Disabled voip-qos-trusted Prioritizes the RTP traffic based on the DSCP value set by the end user device. NOTE: On enabling, all UCC based ALGs will be disabled. Disabled by default.

tunnel-trusted.description:
Changes the role of incoming tunnel traffic in the inbound firewall role to a permit-all role.
Enabling this parameter allows the AP to be remotely managed through the secured tunnel with the VPN concentrator.

voip-wmm-content-enforcement.description: If traffic to or from the user is inconsistent with the associated QoS policy for voice, the traffic is reclassified to best effort and data path counters incremented. This parameter requires the PEFNG license. Disabled by default

wireless-bridge-aging.description: Prevent the age-out of wireless clients when associated to AP. Enabled by default.

indicate a denial of service attack.

deny-needfrag-df-gre.description: Drops IP packets with DF bit set when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is not sent.

deny-needfrag-df-gre-xmit-icmp.description: Drops IP packets with DF bit set when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is sent.

deny-needfrag-df-ipsec.description: Drops IP packets with DF bit set when packet length is greater than IPsec tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is not sent.

deny-needfrag-gre.description: Drops IP packets when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is not sent

deny-needfrag-gre-xmit-icmp.description: Drops IP packets when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU and an ICMP error message is sent.

deny-source-routing.description: Disallow forwarding of IP frames with source routing options set

dhcp-perf-monitoring.description: Enables DHCP performance monitoring. This parameter is used to measure the time taken for a DHCP exchange.

disable-ftp-server.description: Enables DHCP performance monitoring. This parameter is used to measure the time taken for a DHCP exchange.Enabling this option could cause APs to not boot up. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. disabled

drop-ip-fragments.description: When enabled, all IP fragments are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-bridging.description: Enables bridging when the managed device is in factory default

enable-gre-inner-pkt-frag.description: Enables fragmenting inner IP frames when packet length is greater than GRE tunnel MTU.

enable-per-packet-logging.description: Enables logging of every packet if logging is enabled for the corresponding session rule. Normally, one event is logged per session. If you enable this option, each packet in the session is logged. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative, as doing so may create unnecessary overhead on the managed device.

enable-port-packet-drop-logging.description: Enables port packet logging. If enabled, the dropped frames are logged. Disabled by default

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

gre-call-id-processing.description: Creates a unique state for each PPTP tunnel. Do not enable this option unless instructed to do so by a technical support representative.

immediate-feedback.description:
Triggers datapath immediate freeback of buffers.
Applicable to 72xx Platform only(Default disabled)

wired-server-ip.description: IP address of the wired server

items.description: IP address of the wired server
items.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

wired-server-ipv6.description: IPv6 address of the wired server

items.description: IPv6 address of the wired server
items.x-typeDescription:
An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

wired-server-mac.description: MAC address of the wired server

items.description: MAC address of the wired server

ip-classification.description:
Enable IP reputation/geolocation classification (Need
to configure IP name server)

local-valid-users.description: Only allow IP addresses of local subnets in user table

log-icmp-error.description: Logs received ICMP errors. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by a customer support representative. Disabled by default

maximum-threshold.description: Configures maximum threshold. Requires minimum-threshold to be configured. Range 0-100.

optimize-dad-frames.description:
Optimizes DAD(Duplicate Address Detection) frames on wireless - reduce flooding of
IPv4 gratuitous ARPs/IPv6 DAD frames onto wireless clients. Enabled by default

outstanding-buffers-hwm-percent.description: Outstanding Buffers High Water Mark Percentage(1-99)

prevent-dhcp-exhaustion.description: Enable check for DHCP client hardware address against the packet source MAC address. This command checks the frame's source-MAC against the DHCPv4 client hardware address and drops the packet if it does not match. Enabling this feature prevents a client from submitting multiple DHCP requests with different hardware addresses, thereby preventing DHCP pool depletion.

prohibit-arp-spoofing.description: Detects and prohibits arp spoofing. When this option is enabled, possible arp spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent.

prohibit-ip-spoof-all.description: Detects ARP spoofing, when an intruder sends ARP using the IP and MAC addresses of a known user. When this option is enabled, additional check for the user route cache entry is performed. If the route cache entry is not found or if found, but the route cache entry MAC does not match with the sender MAC in ARP frame, then, the frame is marked as spoof. The IP spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent. If the Prohibit IP Spoofing option is enabled, the controller denies all the traffic from the client using an IP address that is used by another client that has an entry in the user table. This option does not allow multiple MAC addresses to use the same IP address.

prohibit-ip-spoofing.description: Detects IP spoofing (where an intruder sends messages using the IP address of a trusted client). When this option is enabled, source and destination IP and MAC addresses are checked; possible IP spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent. Enabled in IPv4, disabled in IPv6.

prohibit-rc-update.description: This option prohibits route-cache update on user add. Disabled by default.

prohibit-rst-replay.description: Closes a TCP connection in both directions if a TCP RST is received from either direction. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Disabled by default

restrict-corp-access.description: Restricted access to the corporate network.

session-idle-timeout.description: Time, in seconds, that a non-TCP session can be idle before it is removed from the session table. You should not modify this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Default value is 16. Valid range 16-300

session-spread.description: Enables counter CPU to perform encryption. Applicable to 7200 Series controllers only Disabled by default

session-tunnel-fib.description:
Enable session,tunnel based forwarding. !!Warning!! It
is recommended to toggle this knob during maintenance
window or OFF peak production hours. On M3 this knob
will enable ONLY tunnel based forwarding, session based
does NOT apply to this platform.

shape-mcast.description: Enables multicast optimization and provides excellent streaming quality regardless of the amount of VLANs or IP IGMP groups that are used. Disabled by default.

stall-crash.description: Triggers datapath crash on stall detection. Applies to the to 7200 Series managed device only

tcp-session-idle-timeout.description: Time, in seconds, that a TCP session can be idle before it is removed from the session table. You should not modify this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Default value is 16. Valid range 300-43200

track-spoof.description: Detects spoof in datapath. Disabled voip-qos-trusted Prioritizes the RTP traffic based on the DSCP value set by the end user device. NOTE: On enabling, all UCC based ALGs will be disabled. Disabled by default.

tunnel-trusted.description:
Changes the role of incoming tunnel traffic in the inbound firewall role to a permit-all role.
Enabling this parameter allows the AP to be remotely managed through the secured tunnel with the VPN concentrator.

voip-wmm-content-enforcement.description: If traffic to or from the user is inconsistent with the associated QoS policy for voice, the traffic is reclassified to best effort and data path counters incremented. This parameter requires the PEFNG license. Disabled by default

wireless-bridge-aging.description: Prevent the age-out of wireless clients when associated to AP. Enabled by default.

attack-rate.description:
List of attack-rate protocol, rate and action configurations. Sets rates which, if exceeded, can
indicate a denial of service attack.

protocol.description: Protocol to monitor for Denial of Service attacks.
protocol.x-typeDescription: Protocol to monitor for ipv6 Denial of Service attacks..

rate.description: Number of requests per 30 seconds. Valid range 1-16384

drop-ip-fragments.description: When enabled, all IP fragments are dropped. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative

enable-per-packet-logging.description: Enables logging of every packet if logging is enabled for the corresponding session rule. Normally, one event is logged per session. If you enable this option, each packet in the session is logged. You should not enable this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative, as doing so may create unnecessary overhead on the managed device.

enable-stateful-icmp.description: Enables stateful ICMP processing. This parameter create sessions for ICMP errors and denies unidirectional replies. Disabled by default.

enforce-tcp-handshake.description: Prevents data from passing between two clients until the three-way TCP handshake has been performed. This option should be disabled when you have mobile clients on the network as enabling this option will cause mobility to fail. You can enable this option if there are no mobile clients on the network.

enforce-tcp-sequence.description: Enforces the TCP sequence numbers for all packets. Disabled by default

ext-hdr-parse-len.description: Set the threshold value beyond which the IPv6 header will not be parsed and the packet will be dropped. Default value 100. Range 100-300

prohibit-ip-spoofing.description: Detects IP spoofing (where an intruder sends messages using the IP address of a trusted client). When this option is enabled, source and destination IP and MAC addresses are checked; possible IP spoofing attacks are logged and an SNMP trap is sent. Enabled in IPv4, disabled in IPv6.

prohibit-rst-replay.description:
Closes a TCP connection in both directions if a TCP RST is
received from either direction. You should not enable this
option unless instructed to do so by a customer support
representative

session-idle-timeout.description: Time, in seconds, that a non-TCP session can be idle before it is removed from the session table. You should not modify this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Default value is 16. Valid range 16-300

tcp-session-idle-timeout.description: Time, in seconds, that a TCP session can be idle before it is removed from the session table. You should not modify this option unless instructed to do so by an Aruba representative. Default value is 16. Valid range 300-43200

flow-offload.description: Configure flow offloading to offload some data processing on AP. By default it's enabled on AOS10, but disabled on AOS8.

ipv6-addr-gen-mode.description: Specifies the method to generate IPv6 address.

x-enumDescriptions.STABLE_PRIVACY: Generate IPv6 address to Stable Privacy.

ipv6-addr-gen-mode.x-typeDescription: Method for the AP to generate IPv6 address.

ipv6-route-gc-interval.description: Kernel route cache garbage collection interval.

ArubaGwCertificateUsage_GwCertificateUsa.description:
Gw-certificate-usage helps to assign server certificates, CA certificates, and
configure revocation checking via CRL or OCSP. Supports primary/secondary revocation
methods with configurable enforcement levels and timeouts. Includes built-in OCSP
responder functionality, certificate groups for multiple VPN scenarios, and separate
certificates for WebUI and captive portal.

profile.description: List of Gateway Certificate Usage profiles

name.description: Name of the Gateway Certificate usage profile to be configured.
name.x-typeDescription: Profile name type. Allowed characters : alphanumeric, hyphen(-), underscore(_). Range : 1-63.

enforcement-level.description: Configure the enforcement policy for when an revocation check cannot be completed.

x-enumDescriptions.FAIL_OVER: Fail over to next RCP method if OCSP Server is unreachable.
x-enumDescriptions.REVOKE_CERT: Assume Certificate is revoked if OCSP Server is unreachable.

ArubaInternalUser_InternalUserConfig.description: Grouping for adding internal RADIUS and Captive portal users.

users.description:
Defines internal users stored on the AP for RADIUS (employee) or captive portal (guest) authentication. Each entry represents
a single user record in the local database. Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete a user entry. Corresponds to the CLI command
‘user <username> <password> <type>’ on APs.

password.description: Secret associated with the username for authentication. Required to add or update a user; without a password, the user cannot log in.

type.description: Select whether the user is for RADIUS (employee) or captive portal (guest) authentication. This determines how the credentials are applied during access.

x-enumDescriptions.PORTAL: Guest/captive portal authentication; user is validated via portal login.
x-enumDescriptions.RADIUS: Employee-style authentication; user is validated for RADIUS access.

username.description: Login name used by the user when authenticating via RADIUS or captive portal. Identifies the user record in the local database.

ArubaInternalUser_InternalUserSchemaGET.description:
Manage the AP's local user database for RADIUS (employee) and captive portal (guest) access.
The local database supports up to 512 users on APs. Use this API to retrieve the list of Internal User profiles.
This feature is only applicable for AP.

profile.description: Internal User profile configuration grouping. Each profile is identified by ‘name’. Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete a profile.

name.description: Profile name for the Internal User configuration.
Profile name for the Internal User configuration.
Profile name for the Internal User configuration.

auth-key.description:
Plaintext key used for authentication.This is
applicable only when key type is PLAIN_TEXT.
auth-key.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

auth-key-ciphertext.description:
Ciphertext key used for authentication. This is
applicable only when key type is CIPHER_TEXT.

auth-key.description:
Plaintext key used for authentication.This is
applicable only when key type is PLAIN_TEXT.

A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

auth-key-ciphertext.description:
Ciphertext key used for authentication. This is
applicable only when key type is CIPHER_TEXT.

deactivated. The accept-end timer must be greater than the accept-start timer.

accept-start.description:

key in the keychain will be active.

crypto-algorithm.description: The type of cryptographic algorithm associated with this key.

recv-id.description: Set the receive ID corresponding to the key instance.

ArubaKeychain_KeychainsSchemaGET.description:
Keychain is a secure mechanism for storing and managing cryptographic keys
and authentication credentials to facilitate secure access and communication
between devices.

address.description: List of MAC addresses that should be locked out.

mac.description: MAC address to be locked out in the form of AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF.

address.description: List of MAC addresses that should be locked out.

mac.description: MAC address to be locked out in the form of AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF.

ArubaMacLockout_MacLockoutSchemaGET.description:
Manage MAC address lockout profiles for Aruba devices, enabling administrators
to block specific MAC addresses from accessing the network. This API allows you
to define and retrieve MAC lockout profiles, specifying which devices are denied
access and for how long. Use this API to retrieve the list of MAC Lockout profiles.

profile.description:
Defines a single MAC Lockout profile, specifying the MAC addresses to be blocked
and the lockout duration. Each entity is identified by name. Use these APIs to create,
modify, or delete an instance.

description.description: User description for MAC lockout profile.

name.description: Unique name for the MAC Lockout profile. Required to reference this profile in device configurations.

description.description: User description for MAC lockout profile.

name.description: Unique name for the MAC Lockout profile. Required to reference this profile in device configurations.

description.description: User description for MAC lockout profile.

name.description: Unique name for the MAC Lockout profile. Required to reference this profile in device configurations.

bypass-list.description: List of packet types to bypass MACsec.

items.description: List of packet types to bypass MACsec.

x-enumDescriptions.IEEE_BPDU:
Bypass MACsec protection for IEEE BPDU frames.
Packets with destination MAC as one of the IEEE BPDU MACs
(01:80:c2:00:00:0*) will bypass the MACsec engine in both the Tx and
Rx directions.

cak.description:
Secure Connectivity Assocation Key (CAK) associated with this
policy.

cipher-suites.description: Configure the cipher-suites to use in the MACsec policy.

items.description: Configure the cipher-suites to use in the MACsec policy.

x-enumDescriptions.GCM_AES_128:
Galois/Counter mode of operation with the AES-128 symmetric block
cipher.
x-enumDescriptions.GCM_AES_128_XPN:
Galois/Counter mode of operation with the AES-128 symmetric block
cipher with extended packet numbering.
x-enumDescriptions.GCM_AES_256:
Galois/Counter mode of operation with the AES-256 symmetric block
cipher.
x-enumDescriptions.GCM_AES_256_XPN:
Galois/Counter mode of operation with the AES-256 symmetric block
cipher with extended packet numbering.

ckn.description:
Connectivity Association Key Name (CKN) associated with this
policy.

clear-tag-mode.description:
Ethernet data in a MACsec PDU that must precede the MACsec SecTAG
in clear text.

x-enumDescriptions.DOT1Q:
Send the 802.1q tag in clear in a MACsec PDU.
When configured, untagged traffic is not allowed on the
MACsec channel.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE:
The SecTAG directly follows the destination and source
MAC address in a MACsec PDU.

confidentiality-enable.description: Enable confidentiality in the MACsec policy.

confidentiality-offset.description:
Number of octets in an Ethernet frame that are unencrypted.
This is only applicable when confidentiality is enabled in the
policy.

x-enumDescriptions.BYTE_0: The entire Ethernet frame is sent encrypted.
x-enumDescriptions.BYTE_30:
The data following the first 30 bytes of the Ethernet
frame is sent encrypted.
x-enumDescriptions.BYTE_50:
The data following the first 50 bytes of the Ethernet
frame is sent encrypted.

data-delay-protection.description:
Enable data delay protection. Data delay protection allows
MKA participants to ensure that the data frames protected by
MACsec delayed by more than 2 seconds are dropped.

include-sci-enable.description:
Enable inclusion of Secure Channel Identifier (SCI) tag
information in the Security TAG (SecTAG) field.

replay-protect-enable.description: Enable replay protection in the MACsec policy.

replay-window.description:
Replay protection window associated with this policy. When a
packet is received, it is processed only if the Packet Number
(PN) associated with this packet is within the replay window.
This is only applicable if the replay protection is enabled in
the policy.
For PVOS device, valid range: 0-1024.
For CX device, valid range: 0-4294967295.
For AP device, valid range: 0-4294967295.

secure-mode.description:
Forwarding behavior of the interface when
the MKA session is not established.

x-enumDescriptions.MUST_SECURE:
Configure the interface to block traffic if MKA session is not
established.
x-enumDescriptions.SHOULD_SECURE:
Configure the interface to allow traffic if MKA session is not
established.

ArubaMacsec_MacsecSchemaGET.description:
MAC Security (MACsec) allows authorized systems that attach to and interconnect
LANs in a network to maintain confidentiality of transmitted data and to take
measures against frames transmitted or modified by unauthorized devices.

cak.description:
Configure Secure Connectivity Association Key (CAK) in plaintext.
This is applicable only when CAK type is PLAIN_TEXT.
cak.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

cak-ciphertext.description:
Configure CAK in ciphertext.This is applicable
only when CAK type is CIPHER_TEXT.

ckn.description:
Connectivity Association Key Name (CKN) associated with this
policy.

cak.description:
Configure Secure Connectivity Association Key (CAK) in plaintext.
This is applicable only when CAK type is PLAIN_TEXT.
cak.x-typeDescription:

Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
