cak-ciphertext.description:
Configure CAK in ciphertext.This is applicable
only when CAK type is CIPHER_TEXT.

ckn.description:
Connectivity Association Key Name (CKN) associated with this
policy.

eapol-destination-mac.description:
The destination MAC address to use in EAPoL packets for MKA.
If not set, the default EAPoL multicast MAC 01:80:c2:00:00:03 is
used.

eapol-dot1q-tagged.description: Enable 802.1q tag in EAPoL frames for MKA.

eapol-eth-type.description:
The Ether-Type to be used in EAPoL packets for MKA.
When not configured, the default Ether-Type 0x888e is used.

key-server-priority.description:
MKA participant priority used to determine which participant is
elected as the key server. A value of 255 indicates that this
participant will not become the key server.

keychain.description: Configure the keychain for MKA policy.

sak-rekey-interval.description: Secure Association Key rekey interval in seconds. Corresponds to 'sak-rekey-interval <seconds>' (AP).

eapol-destination-mac.description:
The destination MAC address to use in EAPoL packets for MKA.
If not set, the default EAPoL multicast MAC 01:80:c2:00:00:03 is
used.

eapol-dot1q-tagged.description: Enable 802.1q tag in EAPoL frames for MKA.

eapol-eth-type.description:
The Ether-Type to be used in EAPoL packets for MKA.
When not configured, the default Ether-Type 0x888e is used.

key-server-priority.description:
MKA participant priority used to determine which participant is
elected as the key server. A value of 255 indicates that this
participant will not become the key server.

keychain.description: Configure the keychain for MKA policy.

sak-rekey-interval.description: Secure Association Key rekey interval in seconds. Corresponds to 'sak-rekey-interval <seconds>' (AP).

ArubaMka_MkaSchemaGET.description:
The MACsec Key Agreement (MKA) protocol facilitates the secure management and
exchange of cryptographic keys for establishing secure Ethernet communications.

ArubaNetGroup_NetGroupsSchemaGET.description:
Configures network group (netdestination) profiles on the
device. A netdestination is a reusable named object that
defines a set of network destinations for use in firewall
policies and session ACLs. When an ACL rule references a
netdestination by name as a source or destination alias,
traffic is matched against all entries in that group.
Each network group can contain entries of type host (single
IP), FQDN (domain name), address-range (IP range), network
(subnet prefix), VLAN (VLAN-based override), port (protocol
port or port range), or interface (tunnel or null interface).
Additional features include inversion (match all destinations
except those in the group) and exclusion (subtract entries
of another netdestination from this group). Supported on
AP, Gateway, SW_CX and SW_PVOS platforms.

net-group.description:
A network group (netdestination) profile. Each profile
is identified by a unique name and contains a list of
network destination entries.

description.description:
A user-defined text description for this network group
profile, used for administrative identification.

exclude.description:
Name of another netdestination profile whose entries should
be excluded (subtracted) from this network group. When
specified, destinations matching the excluded profile are
removed from the effective match set of this group.

invert.description:
When set to true, inverts the match logic of this network
group. Traffic matching any destination NOT in this group
will match the ACL rule referencing this alias. Default
is false (normal match behavior).

items.description:
Ordered list of network destination entries within this
network group profile. Each entry defines a specific
network destination (host, FQDN, range, network, VLAN,
port, or interface) identified by a positional index.

address.description:
A single host IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) for this
network destination entry. Applicable when type is HOST.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

address-range.description:
A contiguous IP address range defined by exactly two
elements: the start address and the end address.
Applicable when type is ADDRESS_RANGE.

items.description:
A contiguous IP address range defined by exactly two
elements: the start address and the end address.
Applicable when type is ADDRESS_RANGE.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

fqdn.description: A Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) applicable when type is FQDN.

index.description:
Positional index of this entry within the network group.
Entries are evaluated in index order. Valid range is
1 to 1000.

network-vlan.description:
The VLAN ID used for network-level VLAN-based destination
matching. Specifies a network VLAN identifier (1-4094).
Applicable when type is VLAN.

ports.description:
Operator and port values. If operator == COMPARISON_RANGE then minimum and maximum values are valid,
otherwise only the minimum value is valid.

max.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

min.description: Port value or lower bound of port range.
min.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

x-enumDescriptions.COMPARISON_RANGE: Minimum and maximum values of range.

prefix.description:
A network subnet specified as an IP prefix (address and
prefix length). Applicable when
type is NETWORK.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 prefix represented in dotted quad notation followed by
a slash and a CIDR mask (0 <= mask <= 32).

An IPv6 prefix represented in full, shortened, or mixed
shortened format followed by a slash and CIDR mask
(0 <= mask <= 128).

type.description:
Configures the type of this network destination entry.
This determines which sibling leaves are applicable
and must be populated: HOST requires 'address', FQDN
requires 'fqdn', ADDRESS_RANGE requires 'address-range',
NETWORK requires 'prefix', VLAN requires 'vlan' or
'network-vlan', PORT requires the 'ports' container,
and INTERFACE requires 'null-interface' or 'tunnel-name'.

x-enumDescriptions.ADDRESS_RANGE:
A contiguous range of IP addresses defined by a start
and end address.
x-enumDescriptions.FQDN: A Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) entry.
x-enumDescriptions.HOST: A single host IP address entry in the netdestination profile.
x-enumDescriptions.NETWORK: A network subnet defined by an IP address and prefix length.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN:
A VLAN-based network destination override. Allows matching
traffic based on VLAN ID with an optional host offset.

type.x-typeDescription:
Network group entry type. Defines the kind of network
destination configured in a netdestination profile.

vlan.description:
The VLAN ID for a host-level VLAN override entry.
Used to match traffic based on the host's VLAN
assignment (1-4094). Applicable when type is VLAN.

vlan-offset.description:
An offset value within the VLAN subnet, used to identify
a specific host address within the VLAN. Valid range is
1 to 254. Applicable when type is VLAN.

name.description:
Configures the unique name for this network group
(netdestination) profile. This leaf is mandatory and
serves as the key for the net-group list. The name is
used to reference this group as a source or destination
alias in session ACLs and firewall policies. Must be
between 1 and 63 characters.

netdestination-type.description:
The IP address family type for this netdestination profile.
Indicates whether entries are IPv4-only or IPv6-only.

x-enumDescriptions.IPV4_IPV6_MIXED:
The netdestination profile contains a mix of both IPv4 and
IPv6 address entries. This is not supported on any devices.
x-enumDescriptions.IPV4_ONLY: The netdestination profile contains only IPv4 address entries.
x-enumDescriptions.IPV6_ONLY: The netdestination profile contains only IPv6 address entries.

netdestination-type.x-typeDescription:
IP address family of the netdestination profile.
Indicates whether the profile contains IPv4-only,
IPv6-only entries.

description.description:
A user-defined text description for this network group
profile, used for administrative identification.

exclude.description:

specified, destinations matching the excluded profile are

ArubaPasspointIdentity_PasspointIdentity.description:
This is used to define a Network Access Identifier (NAI) realm information that
can be sent as an Access network Query Protocol (ANQP) information element in a GAS query response.
The settings configured in this profile determine the NAI realm elements that are included as part of a GAS Response frame.

profile.description:
List of NAI realms whose information can be sent as
an ANQP information element in a GAS query response.

anqp-3gpp.description: List of 3GPP Cellular Network for hotspots that have roaming relationships with cellular operators.

plmn.description: The Public Land Mobile Networks (PLMN) value of the highest-priority network.

items.description: The Public Land Mobile Networks (PLMN) value of the highest-priority network.

anqp-roam-cons.description: List Roaming Consortium OI information be sent in an ANQP information element in a GAS query response.

anqp-roam-cons-name.description: ANQP Roaming Consortium OI information profile name.

org-id.description:
Sends the specified roaming consortium OI in a GAS query response.
The OI must be a hexadecimal value.

auth-param.description: Use the nai-realm-auth-id to send the one of the nai_realm_auth_id methods for the primary NAI Realm ID.

id.x-typeDescription: The enum define of hotspot nai-realm-auth-id.

id-value.description:
key for module. This key is the combination of id and value separated by ~
id        - NAI Realm Authentication Parameter ID.
value     - NAI Realm Authentication Parameter value.

value.description:
NAI Realm Authentication Parameter value. The value need to pair to auth-id:
auth-id                      | auth-value scoped
-----------------------------|-------------------
RESERVED_NAI_REALM_AUTH_ID   | All nai_realm_auth_value
EXPANDED-EAP                 | All nai_realm_auth_value
NON-EAP-INNER-AUTH           | nai_realm_auth_value should in [NON_EAP_RESERVED - NON-EAP-MSCHAPV2]
EAP-INNER-AUTH               | nai_realm_auth_value should in [EAP-IDENTITY - EAP-PEAP-MSCHAPV2]
EXP-INNER-EAP                | All nai_realm_auth_value
CREDENTIAL                   | nai_realm_auth_value should in [CRED-SIM - CRED-VENDOR-SPEC]
TUNNELED-EAP-CREDENTIAL-TYPE | nai_realm_auth_value should in [TUN-CRED-SIM - TUN-CRED-VENDOR-SPEC]
value.x-typeDescription: The enum define of hotspot nai-realm-auth-value.

index.description: Index of EAP Auth Method list.

method.description: Select one of the options to identify the EAP authentication method supported by the hotspot2 Realm.
method.x-typeDescription: The enum define of hotspot nai-realm-eap-method.

enable.description: Enable this profile.

enable-alias.description:
The user may apply an alias named 'enable-alias' for device specific practical config when no enable
is configured, allowing the user to control the profile enablement.

encoding.description:
Issue this command if the NAI Realm named
defined by nai-realm-name <nai-realm-name> is
a UTF-8 formatted character string that is not
formatted in accordance with IETF RFC 4282.
encoding.x-typeDescription: The enum define of hotspot nai-realm-enc.

home-realm.description: Marks the Realm in this profile as the NAI Home Realm.

identity-type.description: Type of authentication used for AirPass.

nai-realm-name.description: List of names for the NAI realm. The realm name is often the domain name of the service provider.

value.description: Value of the NAI realm name.

anqp-3gpp.description: List of 3GPP Cellular Network for hotspots that have roaming relationships with cellular operators.

plmn.description: The Public Land Mobile Networks (PLMN) value of the highest-priority network.

items.description: The Public Land Mobile Networks (PLMN) value of the highest-priority network.

ArubaPortSecurity_PortSecuritySchemaGET.description:
Port security enables a user to configure each switch port with a
unique list of the MAC addresses of devices that are authorized to
access the network through that port. This security enables individual
ports to detect, prevent, and log attempts by unauthorized devices to
communicate through the switch.

name.description: Name of the port security policy.
Name of the port security policy.
Name of the port security policy.

ArubaRadiusModifiers_AttributeProfile.description:
This feature is to provide configuration commands to the user to include specific attributes in these access-request and the corresponding values of these attributes.
Including these attributes in access-request packets helps administrators assign appropriate access policies to the clients based on these attributes.

framed-ip-address.description:
This command enables the inclusion of the RADIUS attribute Framed-IP-Address (type 8) in RADIUS access request packets for port-access clients.
By default, the IP address assigned to the client is not included in the RADIUS access request packets. They are however included in the accounting packets.

request-type.description: RADIUS request type for Framed-IP-Address. This field can only be set if request-type is AUTHENTICATION.

service-type.description: RADIUS service type for Framed-IP-Address. This field can only be set if service-type is PORT_ACCESS.

x-enumDescriptions.BOTH: Management users and port-access clients authentication/accounting.

nas-identifier.description:
Configure the Network Access Switch Identifier.NAS-Id will be sent in RADIUS access-request and accounting packets, which is used to notify
the source of RADIUS access request. NAS-Id can be a FQDN of the NAS or any unique string to identify the NAS.

request-type.description: RADIUS request type for NAS Identifier.

nas-ip-address.description:
Configuration to include Network Access Switch IP Address attribute in RADIUS access request packet.
By default NAS-IP-Address attribute will not be added in authentication or accounting packet for management users.
Both request-type and service-type should be set to include the NAS-IP-Address Attribute.

service-type.description: RADIUS service type for NAS-IP-Address. This field can only be set if service-type is MANAGEMENT.

x-enumDescriptions.BOTH: Management users and port-access clients authentication/accounting.

x-enumDescriptions.DYNAMIC: VLAN assigned to the client by authentication server.
x-enumDescriptions.STATIC: Native VLAN of the client port.

vsa.description: This configuration allows a user to include specific VSAs in RADIUS request packets for different service types.

vendor.description: Name of the vendor, vsa-type and vsa-value must also be configured.

vendor-vsa-type.description: Key combination of vendor and vsa-type separated by ~

vsa-type.description: RADIUS VSA type, vendor and vsa-value must also be configured.
vsa-type.x-typeDescription: RADIUS VSA vendor specific attribute type.

vsa-value.description: RADIUS VSA value, vendor and vsa-type must also be configured.
vsa-value.x-typeDescription: Value of RADIUS vendor specific attribute value pair.

plain-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

ArubaUbt_UbtSchemaGET.description:
Configuration of UBT (User based Tunnel) Profiles.
This feature creates UBT GRE tunnel between the
switch and the controller when UBT users are
present on the switch. It encapsulates incoming
packets from end-hosts in GRE and forwards them
to the Mobility Controller.The feature is enabled
on a Per User Tunneled Node basis.

profile.description:
A list of global profile(s) for UBT, each
scope (global, site, site-collection, device, device-collection)
can accept only one profile.

client-vid.description:
This vlan is used when mode is LOCAL_VLAN.
If UBT Client VLAN is configured in VLAN_EXTEND mode, it is ignored.
VLAN-ID assigned to UBT clients for tunneling traffic from
switch to controller.
client-vid.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

x-enumDescriptions.LOCAL_VLAN:
Clients are assigned to a VLAN that is local to
the switch and associated with their UBT-role
based VLAN when client traffic reaches the
controller. This mode allows for reducing the
number of VLANs that need to be instantiated on
the switch but can only support VLAN-untagged UBT
clients. The 'client-vid' will be used to
specify the local_vlan that UBT clients are
assigned to.
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN_EXTEND:
Clients are assigned to their UBT-role based
VLAN in the hardware datapath. This mode allows
for UBT to support VLAN-tagged clients like VOIP
phones.

name.description: UBT profile name to be configured.

zone.description: A list of UBT zone specific configuration.

backup-controller-ip.description:
UBT zone's secondary controller's ip address.
Each zone within a profile must have a unique backup-controller ip.
This ip must also be distinct from primary-controller ip within
the same zone and primary-controller ip of other zones
backup-controller-ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

enable.description: Enables/Disables UBT functionality for this zone.

maintenance-period.description:
The user-based tunnel maintenance period is in the range of
1 to 720 hours. The default value of 0 can be used to
disable the maintenance window. If maintenance period is configured
then authenticated clients will be retained in current role in the
event of controller unreachability. Client traffic will be dropped
in switch ingress port. Once controller is reachable, client
traffic flow will be resumed. If controller is not reachable even
after configured maintenance period, clients will be
deauthenticated.

papi-security-key.description:
Shared security key that will be used to encrypt UBT PAPI messages
exchanged between this switch and the controller cluster corresponding
to this zone. When not set, shared security key is not used for
encrypting papi messages.

primary-controller-ip.description:
UBT zone's primary controller's ip address.
Each zone within a profile must have a unique primary-controller ip.
This ip must also be distinct from backup-controller ip within
the same zone and backup-controller ip of other zones
primary-controller-ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

sac-heartbeat-interval.description:
Time interval (in seconds) between successive
heartbeat messages to the Switch Anchor Controller(SAC).

uac-keepalive-interval.description:
Time interval (in seconds) between successive
keep-alive messages sent to the User Anchor Controller(UAC).

wol-vlans.description:
Wake-on-LAN VLAN configuration.
These VLANs need to pre-configured on Switch and Controller.
This is applicable only when mode is set to VLAN_EXTEND.

items.description:
Wake-on-LAN VLAN configuration.
These VLANs need to pre-configured on Switch and Controller.
This is applicable only when mode is set to VLAN_EXTEND.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

zone-name-vrf.description: Key combination of zone-name and vrf separated by ~

get.description:
Configure the paramaters of authentication survivability. The authentication survivability feature requires ClearPass Policy Manager 6.0.2 or later,
and is applicable only when external servers such as RADIUS are configured for the SSID. When enabled, AP authenticates the previously connected clients using
EAP-TLS or MAC authentication even when connectivity to ClearPass Policy Manager is temporarily lost. The authentication survivability feature is not applicable when
a RADIUS server is configured as an internal server. This feature is only applicable for GW.

Use this API to configure external captive portal profiles for guest users. For AP and GW, When the captive portal profile is applied to an SSID or a wired profile, the users connecting
to the SSID or wired network are assigned a role with the captive portal rule. For CX, when the client is 802.1x or MAC authenticated through RADIUS-server, the redirect parameters in this
profile can be applied through user-role, Downloadable User Role and RADIUS Vendor Specific Attribute. This feature is applicable for AP, CX and GW.

get.description:
The 802.11k protocol provides mechanisms for APs and clients to dynamically measure the available radio resources.
In an 802.11k enabled network, APs and clients can send neighbor reports, beacon reports, and link measurement reports to each other.
This allows the APs and clients to take appropriate connection actions.
This includes configuring channel-report, reference of Beacon Report Request profile and RRM IE Profile, etc.

get.description:
IEEE 802.1X is an IEEE Standard for port-based Network Access Control (PNAC).
This standard provides an authentication mechanism to devices wishing to attach
to a LAN or WLAN.
IEEE 802.1X defines the encapsulation of the Extensible Authentication Protocol
(EAP) over IEEE 802, which is known as EAP over LAN (EAPOL).

802.1X authentication involves three entities, a supplicant, an authenticator,
and an authentication server.

*Supplicant*: An entity that wants to get authenticated.
*Authenticator*: A network device, such as an Ethernet switch that authenticates
the Supplicant.
*Authentication Server*: Typically a host running software supporting the RADIUS
and EAP protocols that provides an authentication service to an authenticator.

Until the Supplicant is authenticated, 802.1X Authenticator allows only EAPOL
traffic through the port to which the Supplicant is connected.
Only after the authentication is successful, the authenticator allows normal
traffic from the Supplicant.
List of 802.1X authentication profiles.

put.summary: Add or update an existing 802.1X authentication profile

configuring an EST profile on the device. Certificate Enrollment with EST allows users to use their own PKI instead of the factory or self-signed certificates available on the Instant AP. This enables the user to
have maximum visibility and control over the management of the PKI used and address any issues related to security by themselves in a scaled environment.This feature is applicable for all devices.

get.description:
Firewall monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic
based on predefined security rules. It protects against unauthorized
access, cyberattacks, and malicious threats. Firewall is superset of
several of its submodules which all together help in keeping traffic
secure. This model defines the Aruba firewall profile hierarchy used
by APs and Gateways to enforce stateful packet inspection and
policy-based traffic controls. It organizes firewall configuration
into reusable profiles with separate IPv4 and IPv6 subtrees, and
exposes tunables for session handling, traffic classification, rate
limiting/mitigation controls, multicast handling, and other datapath
behaviors.

delete.description:
A named firewall profile that encapsulates a complete set of IPv4 and
IPv6 firewall policies for a given deployment context. Only a single
firewall profile instance is permitted per scope. Each profile is
identified by a unique name and contains separate IPv4 and IPv6
containers with tunables for session management, traffic classification,
spoofing protection, multicast optimization, and various datapath behaviors.
Profiles can be created, updated, and deleted independently, allowing
administrators to maintain multiple policy sets and apply them to different
device groups or network segments as needed.

get.description:
Configures network group (netdestination) profiles on the
device. A netdestination is a reusable named object that
defines a set of network destinations for use in firewall
policies and session ACLs. When an ACL rule references a
netdestination by name as a source or destination alias,
traffic is matched against all entries in that group.
Each network group can contain entries of type host (single
IP), FQDN (domain name), address-range (IP range), network
(subnet prefix), VLAN (VLAN-based override), port (protocol
port or port range), or interface (tunnel or null interface).
Additional features include inversion (match all destinations
except those in the group) and exclusion (subtract entries
of another netdestination from this group). Supported on
AP, Gateway, SW_CX and SW_PVOS platforms.

delete.description:
A network group (netdestination) profile. Each profile
is identified by a unique name and contains a list of
network destination entries.

parameters.description:
Configures the unique name for this network group
(netdestination) profile. This leaf is mandatory and
serves as the key for the net-group list. The name is
used to reference this group as a source or destination
alias in session ACLs and firewall policies. Must be
between 1 and 63 characters.
parameters.name: name

get.description:
Allow you to manage a configured authentication server to an ordered list in a server group, for AP and GW you can configure server rules to derive a user role,
VLAN ID or VLAN name from attributes returned by the server during authentication.
For CX, A maximum of 32 AAA server-groups are supported. Out of these four are default server-groups (local, none, radius, tacacs) and the rest 28 can be user configured.
For AP, A maximum of 16 AAA server-groups are supported for per device. And all server-groups can be user configured.
This feature is applicable for all devices.

get.description:
Condigure a Stateful 802.1X profile.
This feature when enabled allows the controller to learn the identity and role of a user connected to a third-party AP,
and is useful for authenticating users to networks with APs from multiple vendors. This has been widely used in
SDWAN branch gateways to learn the roles of clients connected to IAP. When any 801.1X authenticator sends RADIUS request to server,
gateway inspects this request and its response from server to learn about the authentication state of the user. Gateway then applies an
identity-based user role to the device
List of stateful 802.1X profiles.

put.summary: Replace profile by name

get.description:
Configuration of UBT (User based Tunnel) Profiles.
This feature creates UBT GRE tunnel between the
switch and the controller when UBT users are
present on the switch. It encapsulates incoming
packets from end-hosts in GRE and forwards them
to the Mobility Controller.The feature is enabled
on a Per User Tunneled Node basis.
